   1from dataclasses import dataclass, asdict, fields,field
   2from typing import Any, TypeAlias, TypeVar, cast
   3from firebase_admin import firestore, credentials
   4from google.cloud.firestore_v1.client import Client as FirestoreClient
   5from google.cloud.firestore_v1.document import DocumentReference, DocumentSnapshot
   6from google.cloud.firestore_v1.query import Query
   7from google.cloud.firestore_v1.query_results import QueryResultsList
   8from google.cloud.firestore_v1.types.write import WriteResult
   9from google.cloud.firestore_v1.base_query import FieldFilter
  10from google.protobuf import timestamp_pb2
  11from email.mime.text import MIMEText

  16@dataclass
  17class Comments:
  18    CommentId: str = ""
  19    ConnectedCommentID: str = ""
  20    ListingID: str = ""
  21    UserID:str = ""
  22    Content: str = ""
  23    CreatedAt: str = ""
  24    Tags: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
  25    def as_dict(self) -> dict:
  26        data = asdict(self)
  27        if data["Tags"] is None:
  28            data["Tags"] = []
  29        return data
  30    @classmethod
  31    def from_dict(cls, dict: dict[str,Any]) -> "Comments":
  32        sanitized = {}
  33        for field in fields(cls):
  34            val = dict.get(field.name)
  35            if val is not None:
  36                sanitized[field.name] = val
  37        return cls(**sanitized)
  38

  47    def from_dict(cls, dict: dict[str,Any]) -> "Landlord":
  48        sanitized = {}

  50            val = dict.get(field.name)
  51            if val is not None:
  52                sanitized[field.name] = val
  53        return cls(**sanitized)

 166            if val is not None:
 167                sanitized[field.name] = val
 168        return cls(**sanitized)

 170
 171# super type alias
 172DataObject: TypeAlias = User | Rating | Landlord | Password | Comments | Listing | Codes | AverageRating
 173
 174T = TypeVar("T", bound=DataObject)
 175class database_manager:
 176    connected: bool = False
 177    fire_store: FirestoreClient
 178
 179    def connect_to_database(self):
 180        """
 181        Connects to database and sets things up internally.
 182
 183        Always run before any other commands
 184        """
 185        if not self.connected:
 186            cred = credentials.Certificate("src/Secrets.json")
 187            firebase_admin.initialize_app(cred)
 188            self.connected = True
 189            self.fire_store = firestore.client()
 190            self.update_all_average_ratings()
 191    
 192    # basic data handeling
 193    def _get_data(self, col:str) -> list[dict[str,Any]]:
 194        """gets data from database. INTERNAL DO NOT USE UNLESS YOU KNOW WHAT YOU'RE DOING
 195
 196        Uses 1 query
 197
 198        Args:
 199            col (str): What collection to get from
 200
 201        Returns:
 202            list[dict[str,Any]]: A list of all object gathered
 203        """
 204        collection = self.fire_store.collection(col)
 205        query = collection.get()
 206        total_data = []
 207        for doc in query:
 208            data = doc.to_dict()
 209            total_data.append(data)
 210        return total_data
 211    
 212    def _get_raw_data(self,col:str) -> QueryResultsList[DocumentSnapshot]:
 213        """gets data from database. INTERNAL DO NOT USE UNLESS YOU KNOW WHAT YOU'RE DOING
 214
 215        Uses 1 query
 216
 217        Args:
 218            col (str): What collection to get from
 219
 220        Returns:
 221            QueryResultsList[DocumentSnapshot]: a list of all of the raw documents in the collection.          
 222        """
 223        collection = self.fire_store.collection(col)
 224        query = collection.get()
 225        return query
 226
 227    
 228    def _push_data(self, data:dict,col:str) -> tuple[timestamp_pb2.Timestamp,DocumentReference]: # type: ignore
 229        """Push data to the database. INTERNAL DO NOT USE
 230
 231        Uses 1 query
 232
 233        Args:
 234            data (dict): raw data to push
 235            col (str): what collection it should go to
 236
 237        Returns:
 238            tuple[timestamp_pb2.Timestamp,DocumentReference]: a typle containing when it was pushed and where it was pushed to
 239        """
 240        collection = self.fire_store.collection(col)
 241        return collection.add(data)
 242    
 243    def _unwrap_query(self, q:Query) -> list[dict]:
 244        """Forcefully unwraps a query. INTERNAL DO NOT USE UNLESS YOU KNOW WHAT YOU'RE DOING
 245
 246        Uses 0 queries
 247
 248        Args:
 249            q (Query): any firebase query
 250
 251        Returns:
 252            list[dict]: a dictinary list of everything inside that query. can be packed back into types
 253        """
 254        total_data:list[dict] = []
 255        for doc in q.get():
 256            data = doc.to_dict()
 257            if data != None:
 258                total_data.append(data)
 259        return total_data
 260    
 261    def _get_document_using_id(self,collection_to_search:str, Id_type:DataObject, Id_to_look_for:str)-> list[dict]:
 262        """
 263        Searches the specified collection for all documents with the corresponding ID.
 264
 265        Uses 1 query
 266        
 267        Parameters:
 268            collection_to_search (str): The name of the collection to search within.
 269            Id_type (DataObject): The document containing the ID to search for.
 270            Id_to_look_for (str): exact text of cid to look for
 271
 272        Returns:
 273            list: A list of documents that match the specified ID.
 274        """
 275        if self.connected:
 276            collection = self.fire_store.collection(collection_to_search)
 277            match Id_type:
 278                case User():
 279                    query = collection.where("UserID", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 280                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 281                    
 282                case Password():
 283                    query = collection.where("UserID", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 284                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 285                    
 286                case Rating():
 287                    query = collection.where("RatingID", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 288                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 289                    
 290                case Landlord():
 291                    # unique case: users
 292                    if collection_to_search == "Users":
 293                        query = collection.where("ConnectedLL", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 294                    else:
 295                        query = collection.where("LLID", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 296                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 297                    
 298                case Comments():
 299                    query = collection.where("CommentId", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 300                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 301                    
 302                case Listing():
 303                    query = collection.where("ListingID", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 304                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 305                
 306                case Codes():
 307                    query = collection.where("UserID", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 308                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 309                
 310                case AverageRating():
 311                    query = collection.where("ListingID", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 312                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 313                case _:
 314                    raise TypeError(f"Id_source Not Valid Type: {Id_type}, {type(Id_type)}")
 315        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 316
 317    # basic object handeling
 318    def get_object_by_id(self, id:str, object_type:T) -> T:
 319        """get any one object when given its id and its type
 320
 321        Uses 1 query
 322
 323        Args:
 324            id (str): the id of the requested object
 325            object_type (T): the type of the requested object
 326
 327        Raises:
 328            TypeError: invalid id
 329            IOError: not connected
 330
 331        Returns:
 332            T: full object of the given type
 333        """
 334        if self.connected:
 335            match object_type:
 336                case User():
 337                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
 338                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", id).get()
 339                    if len(documents) == 1:
 340                        user  = documents[0].to_dict()
 341                        if user != None:
 342                            return cast(T, User.from_dict(user))
 343                    raise TypeError(f"No one User with UserID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 344                case Password():
 345                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Passwords") 
 346                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", id).get()
 347                    if len(documents) == 1:
 348                        p = documents[0].to_dict()
 349                        if p != None:
 350                            return cast(T, Password.from_dict(p))
 351                    raise TypeError(f"No one Password with UserID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 352                case Comments():
 353                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Comments")
 354                    documents = collection.where("CommentID", "==", id).get()
 355                    if len(documents) == 1:
 356                        c = documents[0].to_dict()
 357                        if c != None:
 358                            return cast(T, Comments.from_dict(c))
 359                    raise TypeError(f"No one Comment with CommentId: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 360                case Landlord():
 361                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Landlord")
 362                    documents = collection.where("LLID", "==", id).get()
 363                    if len(documents) == 1:
 364                        l  = documents[0].to_dict()
 365                        if l != None:
 366                             return cast(T, Landlord.from_dict(l))
 367                    raise TypeError(f"No one Landlord with LLID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 368                case Listing():
 369                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Listing")
 370                    documents = collection.where("LLID", "==", id).get()
 371                    if len(documents) == 1:
 372                        l  = documents[0].to_dict()
 373                        if l != None:
 374                            return cast(T, Listing.from_dict(l))
 375                    raise TypeError(f"No one Listing with ListingID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 376                case Rating():
 377                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Rating")
 378                    documents = collection.where("RatingID", "==", id).get()
 379                    if len(documents) == 1:
 380                        r  = documents[0].to_dict()
 381                        if r != None:
 382                            return cast(T, Rating.from_dict(r))
 383                    raise TypeError(f"No one Rating with RatingID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 384                case Codes():
 385                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Codes")
 386                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", id).get()
 387                    if len(documents) == 1:
 388                        c  = documents[0].to_dict()
 389                        if c != None:
 390                            return cast(T, Codes.from_dict(c))
 391                    raise TypeError(f"No one Code with UserID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 392                case AverageRating():
 393                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
 394                    documents = collection.where("ListingID", "==", id).get()
 395                    if len(documents) == 1:
 396                        ar  = documents[0].to_dict()
 397                        if ar != None:
 398                            return cast(T, AverageRating.from_dict(ar))
 399                    raise TypeError(f"No one AverageRating with ListingID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 400        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 401
 402    def recursive_deletion(self, deleted_object:DataObject) -> bool:
 403        """Recursivly removes all objects connected to the deleated object. including the origional object
 404
 405        Uses 1 query per object deleated.
 406
 407        Args:
 408            deleted_object (DataObject): any data object
 409
 410        Raises:
 411            TypeError: Invalid data type
 412            IOError: not connected
 413
 414        Returns:
 415            bool: weather deleation was sucessfull or not 
 416        """
 417        # recursive so it actually implements castcading removal.
 418        if self.connected:
 419            match deleted_object:
 420                case User():
 421                    # remove main object
 422                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
 423                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", deleted_object.UserID).get()
 424                    if len(documents) == 1:
 425                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 426                    else:
 427                        return False
 428
 429                    # get connected items
 430
 431                        # passwords
 432                    try:
 433                        password = self.get_pass_from_user(deleted_object)
 434                        self.recursive_deletion(password)
 435                    except:
 436                        pass
 437
 438                        # comments
 439                    try:
 440                        comments = self.get_comments_from_user(deleted_object)
 441                        for c in comments:
 442                            self.recursive_deletion(c)
 443                    except:
 444                        pass
 445
 446                        # ratings
 447                    try:
 448                        ratings = self.get_ratings_from_user(deleted_object)
 449                        for r in ratings:
 450                            self.recursive_deletion(r)
 451                    except:
 452                            pass
 453                    
 454                        # codes
 455                    try:
 456                        code = self.get_code_from_user(deleted_object)
 457                        self.recursive_deletion(code)
 458                    except:
 459                            pass
 460                    return True
 461                case Password():
 462                    # remove password and leave.
 463                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Passwords")
 464                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", deleted_object.UserID).get()
 465                    if len(documents) == 1:
 466                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 467                    else:
 468                        return False
 469                    return True               
 470                case Rating():
 471                    # remove rating and leave.
 472                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Rating")
 473                    documents = collection.where("RatingID", "==", deleted_object.RatingID).get()
 474                    if len(documents) == 1:
 475                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 476                    else:
 477                        return False
 478                    return True                
 479                case Landlord():
 480                    # remove main object
 481                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Landlords")
 482                    documents = collection.where("LLID", "==", deleted_object.LLID).get()
 483                    if len(documents) == 1:
 484                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 485                    else:
 486                        return False
 487
 488                    # get connected items
 489                        # Users
 490                    try:
 491                        users = self.get_connected_users_with_landlord(deleted_object)
 492                        for u in users:
 493                            self.recursive_deletion(u)
 494                    except:
 495                            pass
 496                        # listings
 497                    try:
 498                        listings = self.get_connected_listings_with_landlord(deleted_object)
 499                        for l in listings:
 500                            self.recursive_deletion(l)
 501                    except:
 502                            pass
 503                    return True
 504                case Comments():
 505                    # remove comment and leave.
 506                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Comments")
 507                    documents = collection.where("CommentId", "==", deleted_object.CommentId).get()
 508                    if len(documents) == 1:
 509                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 510                    else:
 511                        return False
 512                    return True
 513                case Listing():
 514                    # remove main object
 515                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Listing")
 516                    documents = collection.where("ListingID", "==", deleted_object.ListingID).get()
 517                    if len(documents) == 1:
 518                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 519                    else:
 520                        return False
 521
 522                    # get connected items
 523                        # comments
 524                    try:
 525                        comments = self.get_comments_from_listing(deleted_object)
 526                        for c in comments:
 527                            self.recursive_deletion(c)
 528                    except:
 529                            pass
 530                        # ratings
 531                    try:
 532                        ratings = self.get_ratings_from_listing(deleted_object)
 533                        for r in ratings:
 534                            self.recursive_deletion(r)
 535                    except:
 536                            pass
 537                        # rating average
 538                    #average = 
 539                    return True
 540                case Codes():
 541                    # remove code and leave.
 542                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Codes")
 543                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", deleted_object.UserID).get()
 544                    if len(documents) == 1:
 545                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 546                    else:
 547                        return False
 548                    return True
 549                case AverageRating():
 550                    # remove code and leave.
 551                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
 552                    documents = collection.where("ListingID", "==", deleted_object.ListingID).get()
 553                    if len(documents) == 1:
 554                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 555                    else:
 556                        return False
 557                    return True
 558                
 559                case _:
 560                    raise TypeError(f"Id_source Not Valid Type: {deleted_object}, {type(deleted_object)}")
 561        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 562    
 563    def get_all_from(self, data_class:T) -> list[T]:
 564        """Get all data of any one particular type from the database. 
 565
 566        Uses 1 query
 567
 568        Args:
 569            data_class (T): Any of the valid datatypes. dont use a real object just make a new constructor
 570
 571        Raises:
 572            IOError: _description_
 573
 574        Returns:
 575            list[T]: list of the same type as inputed
 576        """
 577        collection = ""
 578        match data_class:
 579            case User():
 580                collection = "Users"
 581            case Rating():
 582                collection = "Rating"
 583            case Landlord():
 584                collection = "Landlords"
 585            case Password():
 586                collection = "Passwords"
 587            case Comments():
 588                collection = "Comments"
 589            case Listing():
 590                collection = "Listing"
 591            case Codes():
 592                collection = "Codes"
 593            case AverageRating():
 594                collection = "AverageRating"
 595
 596        if self.connected:
 597            datalist = self._get_data(collection)
 598            return cast(list[T], [data_class.from_dict(i) for i in datalist])
 599        else:
 600            raise IOError("Not Connected")
 601
 602    def add_object(self,object:DataObject) -> None:
 603        """Adds an object into the database
 604
 605        Uses 1 query
 606
 607        Args:
 608            object (DataObject): any dataobject
 609
 610        Raises:
 611            IOError: not connected to the database
 612        """
 613        collection = ""
 614        match object:
 615            case User():
 616                collection = "Users"
 617            case Rating():
 618                collection = "Rating"
 619            case Landlord():
 620                collection = "Landlords"
 621            case Password():
 622                collection = "Passwords"
 623            case Comments():
 624                collection = "Comments"
 625            case Listing():
 626                collection = "Listing"
 627            case Codes():
 628                collection = "Codes"
 629            case AverageRating():
 630                collection = "AverageRating"
 631        if self.connected:
 632            result = self._push_data(object.as_dict(),collection)
 633        else:
 634            raise IOError("Not Connected")
 635        
 636    def update_object(self, object:DataObject) -> WriteResult:
 637        """Updates object in database. Use if the object was modified in any way
 638
 639        Uses 2 queries. One to get and 1 to update.
 640
 641        Args:
 642            object (DataObject): object to update.
 643
 644        Raises:
 645            TypeError: Object does not exsist in the database. Invalid id
 646            IOError: not connected to the database
 647
 648        Returns:
 649            WriteResult: write result. not usefull in most cases. ignore
 650        """
 651        if self.connected:
 652            match object:
 653                case User():
 654                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
 655                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", object.UserID).get()
 656                    if len(documents) == 1:
 657                        refrence = documents[0]
 658                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 659                    raise TypeError(f"No one User with UserID: {object.UserID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 660                case Password():
 661                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Passwords")
 662                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", object.UserID).get()
 663                    if len(documents) == 1:
 664                        refrence = documents[0]
 665                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 666                    raise TypeError(f"No one Password with UserID: {object.UserID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 667                case Comments():
 668                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Comments")
 669                    documents = collection.where("CommentID", "==", object.CommentId).get()
 670                    if len(documents) == 1:
 671                        refrence = documents[0]
 672                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 673                    raise TypeError(f"No one Comment with CommentId: {object.CommentId}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 674                case Landlord():
 675                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Landlord")
 676                    documents = collection.where("LLID", "==", object.LLID).get()
 677                    if len(documents) == 1:
 678                        refrence = documents[0]
 679                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 680                    raise TypeError(f"No one Landlord with LLID: {object.LLID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 681                case Listing():
 682                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Listing")
 683                    documents = collection.where("ListingID", "==", object.ListingID).get()
 684                    if len(documents) == 1:
 685                        refrence = documents[0]
 686                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 687                    raise TypeError(f"No one Listing with ListingID: {object.ListingID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 688                case Rating():
 689                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Rating")
 690                    documents = collection.where("RatingID", "==", object.RatingID).get()
 691                    if len(documents) == 1:
 692                        refrence = documents[0]
 693                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 694                    raise TypeError(f"No one Rating with RatingID: {object.RatingID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 695                case Codes():
 696                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Codes")
 697                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", object.UserID).get()
 698                    if len(documents) == 1:
 699                        refrence = documents[0]
 700                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 701                    raise TypeError(f"No one Code with UserID: {object.UserID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 702                case AverageRating():
 703                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
 704                    documents = collection.where(filter=FieldFilter("ListingID", "==", object.ListingID)).get()
 705                    if len(documents) == 1:
 706                        refrence = documents[0]
 707                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 708                    raise TypeError(f"No one AverageRating with ListingID: {object.ListingID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 709        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 710
 711    def update_average_rating(self, listing: Listing):
 712        """Updates the average rating of 1 specific listing
 713
 714        Uses 3 queries.
 715
 716        Args:
 717            listing (Listing): any listing
 718        """
 719        # get all ratings for listing
 720        # average
 721        ratings = self.get_ratings_from_listing(listing) # 1 query, required
 722
 723        sum = 0
 724        count = len(ratings)
 725        for rating in ratings:
 726            sum += rating.Rating
 727        average = sum/count if count != 0 else 0
 728
 729        ar = AverageRating(listing.ListingID, average, count)
 730        ref = self.get_average_rating_ref(listing) # 1 query
 731        
 732        if ref is not None:  
 733            collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
 734            print("average rating refrence:",ref.id)
 735            collection.document(ref.id).update(ar.as_dict()) # 1 query
 736        else:
 737            self.add_object(ar)  # 1 query
 738
 739    def update_all_average_ratings(self):
 740        """Updates the average rating of all listings
 741
 742        Uses 3 queries. + up to L updates
 743        """
 744        # i can do this in 3
 745        ratings = self.get_all_from(Rating())
 746        listings = self.get_all_from(Listing())
 747        current_averages = self.get_all_from(AverageRating())
 748
 749        # sort by listing
 750        ratings_per_listing:dict[str, list[Rating]] = {}
 751        for r in ratings:
 752            if r.ListingID in ratings_per_listing:
 753                ratings_per_listing[r.ListingID].append(r)
 754                continue
 755            ratings_per_listing[r.ListingID] = [r]
 756
 757        known_averages:dict[str, AverageRating] = {}
 758        for avg in current_averages:
 759            known_averages[avg.ListingID] = avg
 760        
 761        for l in listings: # for n listings
 762            try:
 763                ratings = ratings_per_listing[l.ListingID]
 764            except:
 765                rating = []
 766
 767            sum = 0
 768            count = len(ratings)
 769            for rating in ratings:
 770                sum += rating.Rating
 771            average = sum/count if count != 0 else 0
 772            if l.ListingID in known_averages:
 773                if average != known_averages[l.ListingID].AverageRating:
 774                    self.update_object(AverageRating(l.ListingID, average, count))
 775            else:
 776                self.add_object(AverageRating(l.ListingID, average, count))
 777            
 778    # Email functions
 779
 780    def send_code(self, user: User,code:Codes) -> bool:
 781        """Send an email to any user's email address
 782
 783        Args:
 784            user (User): The user we are sending the code to
 785            code (Codes): The code we are sending
 786
 787        Returns:
 788            bool: Whether the email was sent properly or not
 789
 790        Uses 0 queries.
 791        """
 792        subject = "RateMyHousingCode"
 793        body = f"Your code is {code.Code}"
 794        with open("src/Secrets2.json") as f:
 795            secrets = json.decoder.JSONDecoder().decode("".join(f.readlines()))
 796        sender = secrets["Sender"]
 797        password = secrets["Password"]
 798
 799        msg = MIMEText(body)
 800        msg['Subject'] = subject
 801        msg['From'] = sender
 802        msg['To'] = user.Email
 803        with smtplib.SMTP_SSL('smtp.gmail.com', 465) as smtp_server:
 804            smtp_server.login(sender, password)
 805            smtp_server.sendmail(sender, user.Email, msg.as_string())
 806            print(f"Message sent to {user.Email}")
 807            return True
 808        return False
 809    
 810    def verify_code(self, user: User,code: int) -> bool:
 811        """Verifies any code send from user is the valid code
 812
 813        Args:
 814            user (User): User who's code we are checking
 815            code (int): the code we want to varify
 816
 817        Returns:
 818            bool: true if the code is valid
 819
 820        Uses 2 queries.
 821        """
 822        if self.check_for_code(user):
 823            c = self.get_code_from_user(user)
 824            return c.Code == code
 825        return False
 826
 827    # check for x functions
 828    
 829    def check_for_username(self, username:str) -> bool:
 830        """Uses username to check for a valid user
 831
 832        Args:
 833            username (str): username to check
 834
 835        Raises:
 836            IOError: if not connected to database
 837
 838        Returns:
 839            bool: valid user or not
 840
 841        Uses 1 query.
 842        """
 843        if self.connected:
 844            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
 845            query = collection.where("Username","==",username)
 846            user = [doc.to_dict() for doc in query.get()]
 847            return len(user) >= 1
 848        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 849   
 850    def check_for_email(self, email:str) -> bool:
 851        """Checks to see if an email belongs to a user or not
 852
 853        Args:
 854            email (str): email to check
 855
 856        Raises:
 857            IOError: if not connected to database
 858
 859        Returns:
 860            bool: valid or not
 861
 862        Uses 1 query.
 863        """
 864        if self.connected:
 865            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
 866            query = collection.where("Email","==",email)
 867            user = [doc.to_dict() for doc in query.get()]
 868            return len(user) >= 1
 869        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 870
 871    def get_average_rating_ref(self, listing: Listing) -> DocumentSnapshot | None:
 872        """gets the average rating refrences from any listing
 873
 874        Args:
 875            listing (Listing): Listing to target
 876
 877        Raises:
 878            IOError: if not connected to database
 879
 880        Returns:
 881            DocumentSnapshot | None: the snapshot of the document that represents the average rating
 882        """
 883        if self.connected:
 884            collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
 885            query = collection.where("ListingID","==",listing.ListingID)
 886            test = query.get()
 887            if len(test) >= 1:
 888                return test[0]
 889            else:
 890                return None
 891        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 892    
 893    def check_for_code(self, user: User) -> bool:
 894        """checks if a user has a code or not
 895
 896        Args:
 897            user (User): The user to check for
 898
 899        Raises:
 900            IOError: If not connected to database
 901
 902        Returns:
 903            bool: true if code exists
 904
 905        Uses 1 query.
 906        """
 907        if self.connected:
 908            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Codes")
 909            query = collection.where("UserID","==",user.UserID)
 910            test = self._unwrap_query(query)
 911            if len(test) >= 1:
 912                return True
 913            else:
 914                return False
 915        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 916    
 917
 918    # mod tools
 919    def find_orphend_data(self, data_type:T) -> list[T]:
 920        """finds all orphens in any particular datatype
 921
 922        Args:
 923            data_type (T): The datatype to find orphens for
 924
 925        Returns:
 926            list[T]: A list of all ophens
 927
 928        Uses 1+n queries.
 929        """
 930        orphens = []
 931        match data_type:
 932            case User():
 933                # dependent on password
 934                for user in self.get_all_from(User()):
 935                    try: self.get_pass_from_user(user)
 936                    except: orphens.append(user)
 937            case Rating():
 938                # dependent on user and listing
 939                for rating in self.get_all_from(Rating()):
 940                    try: self.get_user_from_rating(rating)
 941                    except: 
 942                        orphens.append(rating)
 943                        break
 944                    try: self.get_listing_from_rating(rating)
 945                    except: orphens.append(rating)
 946            case Landlord():
 947                # dependent on nothing
 948                pass
 949            case Password():
 950                # dependent on user
 951                for password in self.get_all_from(Password()):
 952                    try: self.get_object_by_id(password.UserID,User())
 953                    except: orphens.append(password)
 954            case Comments():
 955                # dependent on user and Listing
 956                for com in self.get_all_from(Comments()):
 957                    try: self.get_listing_from_comments(com)
 958                    except: 
 959                        orphens.append(com)
 960                        break
 961                    try: self.get_user_from_comments(com)
 962                    except: 
 963                        orphens.append(com)
 964            case Listing():
 965                # dependent on lanloard
 966                for listing in self.get_all_from(Listing()):
 967                    try: self.get_landlord_from_Listing(listing)
 968                    except: orphens.append(listing)
 969            case Codes():
 970                # dependent on user
 971                for code in self.get_all_from(Codes()):
 972                    try: self.get_object_by_id(code.UserID,User())
 973                    except: orphens.append(code)
 974            case AverageRating():
 975                # dependent on listing
 976                for average in self.get_all_from(AverageRating()):
 977                    try: self.get_object_by_id(average.ListingID,Listing())
 978                    except: orphens.append(average)
 979            case _:
 980                    pass
 981        return orphens
 982
 983    def has_missing_data(self, object:dict, data_type:DataObject)-> tuple[bool, list]:
 984        """checks any onee object to see if it has missing data or not
 985
 986        Args:
 987            object (dict): object to check
 988            data_type (DataObject): the datatype to check this to
 989
 990        Returns:
 991            tuple[bool, list]: returns wether the object has missing data or not and where the missing data is
 992
 993        Uses 0 queries
 994        """
 995        missing_keys = []
 996        result = False
 997        for field in fields(data_type):
 998            if field not in object:
 999                result = True
1000                missing_keys.append(field)
1001        return result, missing_keys
1002    
1003
1004
1005    # User relations
1006    # username -> user
1007    # email -> user
1008    # user -> password
1009    # User <-> lanloard
1010    # user <-> rating (many)
1011    # user <-> comments (many)
1012    # User -> Code
1013    def get_user_with_username(self, username:str) -> User:
1014        """With a username get a user 
1015
1016        Args:
1017            username (str): username to intaragate
1018
1019        Raises:
1020            TypeError: no user with that username
1021            IOError: if not connected with database
1022
1023        Returns:
1024            User: user with that username
1025
1026        Uses 1 query.
1027        """
1028        if self.connected:
1029            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
1030            query = collection.where("Username","==",username)
1031            user = self._unwrap_query(query)
1032            if len(user) == 1:
1033                user = user[0]
1034                return User.from_dict(user)
1035            else:
1036                raise TypeError(f"no user with the username: {username}")
1037        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1038    
1039    def get_user_with_email(self, email:str) -> User:
1040        """Get user with email
1041
1042        Args:
1043            email (str): email to look for
1044
1045        Raises:
1046            TypeError: no user with that email
1047            IOError: if not connected with database
1048
1049        Returns:
1050            User: user with that email
1051
1052        Uses 1 query.
1053        """
1054        if self.connected:
1055            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
1056            query = collection.where("Email","==",email)
1057            user = self._unwrap_query(query)
1058            if len(user) == 1:
1059                user = user[0]
1060                return User.from_dict(user)
1061            else:
1062                raise TypeError(f"no user with the email: {email}")
1063        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1064
1065    def get_pass_from_user(self, user:User) -> Password:
1066        """Get password from a user
1067
1068        Args:
1069            user (User): user in question
1070
1071        Raises:
1072            TypeError: no password with that userid
1073            IOError: if not connected to database
1074
1075        Returns:
1076            Password: the (hashed) password connected to the user
1077
1078        Uses 1 query.
1079        """
1080        if self.connected:
1081            password_list = self._get_document_using_id("Passwords",User(),user.UserID)
1082            if len(password_list) == 1:
1083                p = password_list[0]
1084                return Password.from_dict(p)
1085            else:
1086                raise TypeError(f"no password with userid: {user.UserID}")
1087        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1088    
1089    def get_landlord_from_user(self, user:User) -> Landlord:
1090        """get landlord connected to a particular user
1091
1092        Args:
1093            user (User): user to get LL of
1094
1095        Raises:
1096            TypeError: user has no LLid or no LL with that LLid
1097            IOError: if not connected to database
1098
1099        Returns:
1100            Landlord: landlord connected to that user
1101
1102        Uses 1 query.
1103        """
1104        if self.connected:
1105            if user.ConnectedLL ==  "":
1106                raise TypeError(f"User has no LLID")
1107            Landlord_list = self._get_document_using_id("Landlords", Landlord(),user.ConnectedLL)
1108            if len(Landlord_list) == 1:
1109                l = Landlord_list[0]
1110                return Landlord.from_dict(l)
1111            else:
1112                raise TypeError(f"no Landlord with LLID: {user.ConnectedLL}")
1113        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1114
1115    def get_ratings_from_user(self, user:User) -> list[Rating]:
1116        """get all ratings origionating from that user
1117
1118        Args:
1119            user (User): The user to inspect
1120
1121        Raises:
1122            IOError: if not connected to database
1123
1124        Returns:
1125            list[Rating]: a list of all ratings made by that user 
1126
1127        Uses 1 query.
1128        """
1129        if self.connected:
1130            ratings = self._get_document_using_id("Rating", User(),user.UserID)
1131            return [Rating.from_dict(r) for r in ratings]
1132        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1133    
1134    def get_comments_from_user(self, user:User) -> list[Comments]:
1135        """get comments made by a specific user
1136
1137        Args:
1138            user (User): user to inspect
1139
1140        Raises:
1141            IOError: if not connected to database
1142
1143        Returns:
1144            list[Comments]: list of all comment sconnected to this user
1145
1146        Uses 1 query.
1147        """
1148        if self.connected:
1149            coms = self._get_document_using_id("Comments", User(),user.UserID)
1150            return [Comments.from_dict(c) for c in coms]
1151        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1152    
1153    def get_code_from_user(self, user:User) -> Codes:
1154        """get a code from a user
1155
1156        Args:
1157            user (User): user to get code from
1158
1159        Raises:
1160            TypeError: code does not exsist
1161            IOError: if not connected to that database
1162
1163        Returns:
1164            Codes: the activation code for that user
1165
1166        Uses 1 query.
1167        """
1168        if self.connected:
1169            code_list = self._get_document_using_id("Codes", Codes(),user.UserID)
1170            if len(code_list) == 1:
1171                c = code_list[0]
1172                return Codes.from_dict(c)
1173            else:
1174                raise TypeError(f"no code with userid: {user.UserID}")
1175        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1176    
1177    # rating relationships
1178    # Rating <-> user
1179    # Rating <-> Listing
1180    def get_user_from_rating(self,rating:Rating) -> User:
1181        """get user from rating
1182
1183        Args:
1184            rating (Rating): rating to inspect
1185
1186        Raises:
1187            TypeError: invalid username
1188            IOError: if not connected to database
1189
1190        Returns:
1191            User: user who posted the rating
1192
1193        Uses 1 query.
1194        """
1195        if self.connected:
1196            users = self._get_document_using_id("Users",User(),rating.UserID)
1197            if len(users) == 1:
1198                u = users[0]
1199                return User.from_dict(u)
1200            else:
1201                raise TypeError(f"no username with userid: {rating.UserID}")
1202        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1203    
1204    def get_listing_from_rating(self,rating:Rating) -> Listing:
1205        """get listing connected to rating
1206
1207        Args:
1208            rating (Rating): rating to inspect
1209
1210        Raises:
1211            TypeError: invalid listing
1212            IOError: if not connect to database
1213
1214        Returns:
1215            Listing: listing that rating is on
1216
1217        Uses 1 query.
1218        """
1219        if self.connected:
1220            listings = self._get_document_using_id("Listing",Listing(),rating.ListingID)
1221            if len(listings) == 1:
1222                l = listings[0]
1223                return Listing.from_dict(l)
1224            else:
1225                raise TypeError(f"no listing with ListingID: {rating.ListingID}")
1226        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1227    
1228    # comments relationships
1229    # comment <-> user
1230    # comment -> comment
1231    # comment <-> listing
1232    def get_user_from_comments(self,comment:Comments) -> User:
1233        """get user from comments
1234
1235        Args:
1236            comment (Comments): the comment to investigate
1237
1238        Raises:
1239            TypeError: invalid userid
1240            IOError: if not connected to database
1241
1242        Returns:
1243            User: the user who made the comment
1244
1245        Uses 1 query.
1246        """
1247        if self.connected:
1248            users = self._get_document_using_id("Users",User(),comment.UserID)
1249            if len(users) == 1:
1250                u = users[0]
1251                return User.from_dict(u)
1252            else:
1253                raise TypeError(f"no username with userid: {comment.UserID}")
1254        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1255    
1256    def get_comments_from_comments(self,comment:Comments) -> Comments:
1257        """get a connected comment from comment a
1258
1259        Args:
1260            comment (Comments): the comment to start at
1261
1262        Raises:
1263            TypeError: no valid connected comment 
1264            IOError: if not connected to database
1265
1266        Returns:
1267            Comments: a comment that "replys" to the current comment
1268
1269        Uses 1 query.
1270        """
1271        if self.connected:
1272            com = self._get_document_using_id("Comments",Comments(),comment.ConnectedCommentID)
1273            if len(com) == 1:
1274                c = com[0]
1275                return Comments.from_dict(c)
1276            else:
1277                raise TypeError(f"no comment with comment: {comment.ConnectedCommentID}")
1278        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1279    
1280    def get_listing_from_comments(self,comment:Comments) -> Listing:
1281        """get the listing the comment is on
1282
1283        Args:
1284            comment (Comments): comment to invistigate
1285
1286        Raises:
1287            TypeError: listing is not real
1288            IOError: if not connected to database
1289
1290        Returns:
1291            Listing: listing comment is connected to
1292
1293        Uses 1 query.
1294        """
1295        if self.connected:
1296            listings = self._get_document_using_id("Listing",Listing(),comment.ListingID)
1297            if len(listings) == 1:
1298                l = listings[0]
1299                return Listing.from_dict(l)
1300            else:
1301                raise TypeError(f"no listing with ListingID: {comment.ListingID}")
1302        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1303    
1304
1305    # Listing relationships
1306    # listing -> landlord
1307    # listing <-> Rating (many)
1308    # listing <-> Comments (many)
1309    # listing -> Average rating
1310    def get_landlord_from_Listing(self, listing:Listing) -> Landlord:
1311        """get the landlord who made a specific listing
1312
1313        Args:
1314            listing (Listing): the listing in question
1315
1316        Raises:
1317            TypeError: invalid landlord
1318            IOError: if not connected to database
1319
1320        Returns:
1321            Landlord: landlord connected to this listing
1322
1323        Uses 1 query.
1324        """
1325        if self.connected:
1326            Landlord_list = self._get_document_using_id("Landlords", Landlord(),listing.LLID)
1327            if len(Landlord_list) == 1:
1328                l = Landlord_list[0]
1329                return Landlord.from_dict(l)
1330            else:
1331                raise TypeError(f"no Landlord with LLID: {listing.LLID}")
1332        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1333    
1334    def get_ratings_from_listing(self, listing:Listing) -> list[Rating]:
1335        """get all ratings from a specific listing
1336
1337        Args:
1338            listing (Listing): listing to investigate
1339
1340        Raises:
1341            IOError: if not connected to database
1342
1343        Returns:
1344            list[Rating]: a list of all ratings connected to that listing
1345
1346        Uses 1 query.
1347        """
1348        if self.connected:
1349            ratings = self._get_document_using_id("Rating", Listing(),listing.ListingID)
1350            return [Rating.from_dict(r) for r in ratings]
1351        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1352    
1353    def get_comments_from_listing(self, listing:Listing) -> list[Comments]:
1354        """get all comments that belong to a listing
1355
1356        Args:
1357            listing (Listing): the listing in question
1358
1359        Raises:
1360            IOError: if not connected to database
1361
1362        Returns:
1363            list[Comments]: a list of all comments talking about the server
1364
1365        Uses 1 query.
1366        """
1367        if self.connected:
1368            coms = self._get_document_using_id("Comments", Listing(),listing.ListingID)
1369            return [Comments.from_dict(c) for c in coms]
1370        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1371    
1372    def get_average_rating_from_listing(self, listing:Listing) -> AverageRating:
1373        """gets the average rating from a listing
1374
1375        Args:
1376            listing (Listing): listing to calculate
1377
1378        Raises:
1379            TypeError: non exsistent average rating
1380            IOError: if nor connected to database
1381
1382        Returns:
1383            AverageRating: average rating of that listing
1384
1385        Uses 1 query.
1386        """
1387        if self.connected:
1388            ars = self._get_document_using_id("AverageRating", AverageRating(),listing.ListingID)
1389            if len(ars) == 1:
1390                ar = ars[0]
1391                return AverageRating.from_dict(ar)
1392            else:
1393                raise TypeError(f"no AverageRating with ListingID: {listing.LLID}")
1394        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1395    
1396    # landlord rlationships
1397    # Landlord <-> User (many)
1398    # Landlord <-> Listing (many)
1399    def get_connected_users_with_landlord(self, landlord:Landlord) -> list[User]:
1400        """gets all users connected to that landlord
1401
1402        Args:
1403            landlord (Landlord): landlord in question
1404
1405        Raises:
1406            IOError: if not connected to database
1407
1408        Returns:
1409            list[User]: a full list of all users connected to a landlord
1410
1411        Uses 1 query.
1412        """
1413        if self.connected:
1414            users = self._get_document_using_id("Users", Landlord(),landlord.LLID)
1415            return [User.from_dict(u) for u in users]
1416        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1417    
1418    def get_connected_listings_with_landlord(self, landlord:Landlord) -> list[Listing]:
1419        """get all listings the belong to a specific landlord
1420
1421        Args:
1422            landlord (Landlord): landlord in question
1423
1424        Raises:
1425            IOError: if not connected to database
1426
1427        Returns:
1428            list[Listing]: all listings conneceted to landlord
1429
1430        Uses 1 query.
1431        """
1432        if self.connected:
1433            listings = self._get_document_using_id("Listing", Landlord(),landlord.LLID)
1434            return [Listing.from_dict(l) for l in listings]
1435        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
@dataclass
class Comments: 
17@dataclass
18class Comments:
19    CommentId: str = ""
20    ConnectedCommentID: str = ""
21    ListingID: str = ""
22    UserID:str = ""
23    Content: str = ""
24    CreatedAt: str = ""
25    Tags: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
26    def as_dict(self) -> dict:
27        data = asdict(self)
28        if data["Tags"] is None:
29            data["Tags"] = []
30        return data

32    def from_dict(cls, dict: dict[str,Any]) -> "Comments":
33        sanitized = {}
34        for field in fields(cls):
35            val = dict.get(field.name)
36            if val is not None:
37                sanitized[field.name] = val
38        return cls(**sanitized)

CreatedAt: str = ''

DataObject: TypeAlias = User | Rating | Landlord | Password | Comments | Listing | Codes | AverageRating
class database_manager: 
 176class database_manager:
 177    connected: bool = False
 178    fire_store: FirestoreClient
 179
 180    def connect_to_database(self):
 181        """
 182        Connects to database and sets things up internally.
 183
 184        Always run before any other commands
 185        """
 186        if not self.connected:
 187            cred = credentials.Certificate("src/Secrets.json")
 188            firebase_admin.initialize_app(cred)
 189            self.connected = True
 190            self.fire_store = firestore.client()
 191            self.update_all_average_ratings()
 192    
 193    # basic data handeling
 194    def _get_data(self, col:str) -> list[dict[str,Any]]:
 195        """gets data from database. INTERNAL DO NOT USE UNLESS YOU KNOW WHAT YOU'RE DOING
 196
 197        Uses 1 query
 198
 199        Args:
 200            col (str): What collection to get from
 201
 202        Returns:
 203            list[dict[str,Any]]: A list of all object gathered
 204        """
 205        collection = self.fire_store.collection(col)
 206        query = collection.get()
 207        total_data = []
 208        for doc in query:
 209            data = doc.to_dict()
 210            total_data.append(data)
 211        return total_data
 212    
 213    def _get_raw_data(self,col:str) -> QueryResultsList[DocumentSnapshot]:
 214        """gets data from database. INTERNAL DO NOT USE UNLESS YOU KNOW WHAT YOU'RE DOING
 215
 216        Uses 1 query
 217
 218        Args:
 219            col (str): What collection to get from
 220
 221        Returns:
 222            QueryResultsList[DocumentSnapshot]: a list of all of the raw documents in the collection.          
 223        """
 224        collection = self.fire_store.collection(col)
 225        query = collection.get()
 226        return query
 227
 228    
 229    def _push_data(self, data:dict,col:str) -> tuple[timestamp_pb2.Timestamp,DocumentReference]: # type: ignore
 230        """Push data to the database. INTERNAL DO NOT USE
 231
 232        Uses 1 query
 233
 234        Args:
 235            data (dict): raw data to push
 236            col (str): what collection it should go to
 237
 238        Returns:
 239            tuple[timestamp_pb2.Timestamp,DocumentReference]: a typle containing when it was pushed and where it was pushed to
 240        """
 241        collection = self.fire_store.collection(col)
 242        return collection.add(data)
 243    
 244    def _unwrap_query(self, q:Query) -> list[dict]:
 245        """Forcefully unwraps a query. INTERNAL DO NOT USE UNLESS YOU KNOW WHAT YOU'RE DOING
 246
 247        Uses 0 queries
 248
 249        Args:
 250            q (Query): any firebase query
 251
 252        Returns:
 253            list[dict]: a dictinary list of everything inside that query. can be packed back into types
 254        """
 255        total_data:list[dict] = []
 256        for doc in q.get():
 257            data = doc.to_dict()
 258            if data != None:
 259                total_data.append(data)
 260        return total_data
 261    
 262    def _get_document_using_id(self,collection_to_search:str, Id_type:DataObject, Id_to_look_for:str)-> list[dict]:
 263        """
 264        Searches the specified collection for all documents with the corresponding ID.
 265
 266        Uses 1 query
 267        
 268        Parameters:
 269            collection_to_search (str): The name of the collection to search within.
 270            Id_type (DataObject): The document containing the ID to search for.
 271            Id_to_look_for (str): exact text of cid to look for
 272
 273        Returns:
 274            list: A list of documents that match the specified ID.
 275        """
 276        if self.connected:
 277            collection = self.fire_store.collection(collection_to_search)
 278            match Id_type:
 279                case User():
 280                    query = collection.where("UserID", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 281                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 282                    
 283                case Password():
 284                    query = collection.where("UserID", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 285                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 286                    
 287                case Rating():
 288                    query = collection.where("RatingID", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 289                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 290                    
 291                case Landlord():
 292                    # unique case: users
 293                    if collection_to_search == "Users":
 294                        query = collection.where("ConnectedLL", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 295                    else:
 296                        query = collection.where("LLID", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 297                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 298                    
 299                case Comments():
 300                    query = collection.where("CommentId", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 301                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 302                    
 303                case Listing():
 304                    query = collection.where("ListingID", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 305                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 306                
 307                case Codes():
 308                    query = collection.where("UserID", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 309                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 310                
 311                case AverageRating():
 312                    query = collection.where("ListingID", "==", Id_to_look_for)
 313                    return self._unwrap_query(query)
 314                case _:
 315                    raise TypeError(f"Id_source Not Valid Type: {Id_type}, {type(Id_type)}")
 316        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 317
 318    # basic object handeling
 319    def get_object_by_id(self, id:str, object_type:T) -> T:
 320        """get any one object when given its id and its type
 321
 322        Uses 1 query
 323
 324        Args:
 325            id (str): the id of the requested object
 326            object_type (T): the type of the requested object
 327
 328        Raises:
 329            TypeError: invalid id
 330            IOError: not connected
 331
 332        Returns:
 333            T: full object of the given type
 334        """
 335        if self.connected:
 336            match object_type:
 337                case User():
 338                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
 339                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", id).get()
 340                    if len(documents) == 1:
 341                        user  = documents[0].to_dict()
 342                        if user != None:
 343                            return cast(T, User.from_dict(user))
 344                    raise TypeError(f"No one User with UserID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 345                case Password():
 346                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Passwords") 
 347                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", id).get()
 348                    if len(documents) == 1:
 349                        p = documents[0].to_dict()
 350                        if p != None:
 351                            return cast(T, Password.from_dict(p))
 352                    raise TypeError(f"No one Password with UserID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 353                case Comments():
 354                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Comments")
 355                    documents = collection.where("CommentID", "==", id).get()
 356                    if len(documents) == 1:
 357                        c = documents[0].to_dict()
 358                        if c != None:
 359                            return cast(T, Comments.from_dict(c))
 360                    raise TypeError(f"No one Comment with CommentId: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 361                case Landlord():
 362                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Landlord")
 363                    documents = collection.where("LLID", "==", id).get()
 364                    if len(documents) == 1:
 365                        l  = documents[0].to_dict()
 366                        if l != None:
 367                             return cast(T, Landlord.from_dict(l))
 368                    raise TypeError(f"No one Landlord with LLID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 369                case Listing():
 370                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Listing")
 371                    documents = collection.where("LLID", "==", id).get()
 372                    if len(documents) == 1:
 373                        l  = documents[0].to_dict()
 374                        if l != None:
 375                            return cast(T, Listing.from_dict(l))
 376                    raise TypeError(f"No one Listing with ListingID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 377                case Rating():
 378                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Rating")
 379                    documents = collection.where("RatingID", "==", id).get()
 380                    if len(documents) == 1:
 381                        r  = documents[0].to_dict()
 382                        if r != None:
 383                            return cast(T, Rating.from_dict(r))
 384                    raise TypeError(f"No one Rating with RatingID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 385                case Codes():
 386                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Codes")
 387                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", id).get()
 388                    if len(documents) == 1:
 389                        c  = documents[0].to_dict()
 390                        if c != None:
 391                            return cast(T, Codes.from_dict(c))
 392                    raise TypeError(f"No one Code with UserID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 393                case AverageRating():
 394                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
 395                    documents = collection.where("ListingID", "==", id).get()
 396                    if len(documents) == 1:
 397                        ar  = documents[0].to_dict()
 398                        if ar != None:
 399                            return cast(T, AverageRating.from_dict(ar))
 400                    raise TypeError(f"No one AverageRating with ListingID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 401        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 402
 403    def recursive_deletion(self, deleted_object:DataObject) -> bool:
 404        """Recursivly removes all objects connected to the deleated object. including the origional object
 405
 406        Uses 1 query per object deleated.
 407
 408        Args:
 409            deleted_object (DataObject): any data object
 410
 411        Raises:
 412            TypeError: Invalid data type
 413            IOError: not connected
 414
 415        Returns:
 416            bool: weather deleation was sucessfull or not 
 417        """
 418        # recursive so it actually implements castcading removal.
 419        if self.connected:
 420            match deleted_object:
 421                case User():
 422                    # remove main object
 423                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
 424                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", deleted_object.UserID).get()
 425                    if len(documents) == 1:
 426                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 427                    else:
 428                        return False
 429
 430                    # get connected items
 431
 432                        # passwords
 433                    try:
 434                        password = self.get_pass_from_user(deleted_object)
 435                        self.recursive_deletion(password)
 436                    except:
 437                        pass
 438
 439                        # comments
 440                    try:
 441                        comments = self.get_comments_from_user(deleted_object)
 442                        for c in comments:
 443                            self.recursive_deletion(c)
 444                    except:
 445                        pass
 446
 447                        # ratings
 448                    try:
 449                        ratings = self.get_ratings_from_user(deleted_object)
 450                        for r in ratings:
 451                            self.recursive_deletion(r)
 452                    except:
 453                            pass
 454                    
 455                        # codes
 456                    try:
 457                        code = self.get_code_from_user(deleted_object)
 458                        self.recursive_deletion(code)
 459                    except:
 460                            pass
 461                    return True
 462                case Password():
 463                    # remove password and leave.
 464                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Passwords")
 465                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", deleted_object.UserID).get()
 466                    if len(documents) == 1:
 467                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 468                    else:
 469                        return False
 470                    return True               
 471                case Rating():
 472                    # remove rating and leave.
 473                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Rating")
 474                    documents = collection.where("RatingID", "==", deleted_object.RatingID).get()
 475                    if len(documents) == 1:
 476                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 477                    else:
 478                        return False
 479                    return True                
 480                case Landlord():
 481                    # remove main object
 482                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Landlords")
 483                    documents = collection.where("LLID", "==", deleted_object.LLID).get()
 484                    if len(documents) == 1:
 485                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 486                    else:
 487                        return False
 488
 489                    # get connected items
 490                        # Users
 491                    try:
 492                        users = self.get_connected_users_with_landlord(deleted_object)
 493                        for u in users:
 494                            self.recursive_deletion(u)
 495                    except:
 496                            pass
 497                        # listings
 498                    try:
 499                        listings = self.get_connected_listings_with_landlord(deleted_object)
 500                        for l in listings:
 501                            self.recursive_deletion(l)
 502                    except:
 503                            pass
 504                    return True
 505                case Comments():
 506                    # remove comment and leave.
 507                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Comments")
 508                    documents = collection.where("CommentId", "==", deleted_object.CommentId).get()
 509                    if len(documents) == 1:
 510                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 511                    else:
 512                        return False
 513                    return True
 514                case Listing():
 515                    # remove main object
 516                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Listing")
 517                    documents = collection.where("ListingID", "==", deleted_object.ListingID).get()
 518                    if len(documents) == 1:
 519                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 520                    else:
 521                        return False
 522
 523                    # get connected items
 524                        # comments
 525                    try:
 526                        comments = self.get_comments_from_listing(deleted_object)
 527                        for c in comments:
 528                            self.recursive_deletion(c)
 529                    except:
 530                            pass
 531                        # ratings
 532                    try:
 533                        ratings = self.get_ratings_from_listing(deleted_object)
 534                        for r in ratings:
 535                            self.recursive_deletion(r)
 536                    except:
 537                            pass
 538                        # rating average
 539                    #average = 
 540                    return True
 541                case Codes():
 542                    # remove code and leave.
 543                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Codes")
 544                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", deleted_object.UserID).get()
 545                    if len(documents) == 1:
 546                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 547                    else:
 548                        return False
 549                    return True
 550                case AverageRating():
 551                    # remove code and leave.
 552                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
 553                    documents = collection.where("ListingID", "==", deleted_object.ListingID).get()
 554                    if len(documents) == 1:
 555                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
 556                    else:
 557                        return False
 558                    return True
 559                
 560                case _:
 561                    raise TypeError(f"Id_source Not Valid Type: {deleted_object}, {type(deleted_object)}")
 562        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 563    
 564    def get_all_from(self, data_class:T) -> list[T]:
 565        """Get all data of any one particular type from the database. 
 566
 567        Uses 1 query
 568
 569        Args:
 570            data_class (T): Any of the valid datatypes. dont use a real object just make a new constructor
 571
 572        Raises:
 573            IOError: _description_
 574
 575        Returns:
 576            list[T]: list of the same type as inputed
 577        """
 578        collection = ""
 579        match data_class:
 580            case User():
 581                collection = "Users"
 582            case Rating():
 583                collection = "Rating"
 584            case Landlord():
 585                collection = "Landlords"
 586            case Password():
 587                collection = "Passwords"
 588            case Comments():
 589                collection = "Comments"
 590            case Listing():
 591                collection = "Listing"
 592            case Codes():
 593                collection = "Codes"
 594            case AverageRating():
 595                collection = "AverageRating"
 596
 597        if self.connected:
 598            datalist = self._get_data(collection)
 599            return cast(list[T], [data_class.from_dict(i) for i in datalist])
 600        else:
 601            raise IOError("Not Connected")
 602
 603    def add_object(self,object:DataObject) -> None:
 604        """Adds an object into the database
 605
 606        Uses 1 query
 607
 608        Args:
 609            object (DataObject): any dataobject
 610
 611        Raises:
 612            IOError: not connected to the database
 613        """
 614        collection = ""
 615        match object:
 616            case User():
 617                collection = "Users"
 618            case Rating():
 619                collection = "Rating"
 620            case Landlord():
 621                collection = "Landlords"
 622            case Password():
 623                collection = "Passwords"
 624            case Comments():
 625                collection = "Comments"
 626            case Listing():
 627                collection = "Listing"
 628            case Codes():
 629                collection = "Codes"
 630            case AverageRating():
 631                collection = "AverageRating"
 632        if self.connected:
 633            result = self._push_data(object.as_dict(),collection)
 634        else:
 635            raise IOError("Not Connected")
 636        
 637    def update_object(self, object:DataObject) -> WriteResult:
 638        """Updates object in database. Use if the object was modified in any way
 639
 640        Uses 2 queries. One to get and 1 to update.
 641
 642        Args:
 643            object (DataObject): object to update.
 644
 645        Raises:
 646            TypeError: Object does not exsist in the database. Invalid id
 647            IOError: not connected to the database
 648
 649        Returns:
 650            WriteResult: write result. not usefull in most cases. ignore
 651        """
 652        if self.connected:
 653            match object:
 654                case User():
 655                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
 656                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", object.UserID).get()
 657                    if len(documents) == 1:
 658                        refrence = documents[0]
 659                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 660                    raise TypeError(f"No one User with UserID: {object.UserID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 661                case Password():
 662                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Passwords")
 663                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", object.UserID).get()
 664                    if len(documents) == 1:
 665                        refrence = documents[0]
 666                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 667                    raise TypeError(f"No one Password with UserID: {object.UserID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 668                case Comments():
 669                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Comments")
 670                    documents = collection.where("CommentID", "==", object.CommentId).get()
 671                    if len(documents) == 1:
 672                        refrence = documents[0]
 673                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 674                    raise TypeError(f"No one Comment with CommentId: {object.CommentId}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 675                case Landlord():
 676                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Landlord")
 677                    documents = collection.where("LLID", "==", object.LLID).get()
 678                    if len(documents) == 1:
 679                        refrence = documents[0]
 680                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 681                    raise TypeError(f"No one Landlord with LLID: {object.LLID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 682                case Listing():
 683                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Listing")
 684                    documents = collection.where("ListingID", "==", object.ListingID).get()
 685                    if len(documents) == 1:
 686                        refrence = documents[0]
 687                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 688                    raise TypeError(f"No one Listing with ListingID: {object.ListingID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 689                case Rating():
 690                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Rating")
 691                    documents = collection.where("RatingID", "==", object.RatingID).get()
 692                    if len(documents) == 1:
 693                        refrence = documents[0]
 694                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 695                    raise TypeError(f"No one Rating with RatingID: {object.RatingID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 696                case Codes():
 697                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Codes")
 698                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", object.UserID).get()
