 699                    if len(documents) == 1:
 700                        refrence = documents[0]
 701                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 702                    raise TypeError(f"No one Code with UserID: {object.UserID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 703                case AverageRating():
 704                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
 705                    documents = collection.where(filter=FieldFilter("ListingID", "==", object.ListingID)).get()
 706                    if len(documents) == 1:
 707                        refrence = documents[0]
 708                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
 709                    raise TypeError(f"No one AverageRating with ListingID: {object.ListingID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
 710        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 711
 712    def update_average_rating(self, listing: Listing):
 713        """Updates the average rating of 1 specific listing
 714
 715        Uses 3 queries.
 716
 717        Args:
 718            listing (Listing): any listing
 719        """
 720        # get all ratings for listing
 721        # average
 722        ratings = self.get_ratings_from_listing(listing) # 1 query, required
 723
 724        sum = 0
 725        count = len(ratings)
 726        for rating in ratings:
 727            sum += rating.Rating
 728        average = sum/count if count != 0 else 0
 729
 730        ar = AverageRating(listing.ListingID, average, count)
 731        ref = self.get_average_rating_ref(listing) # 1 query
 732        
 733        if ref is not None:  
 734            collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
 735            print("average rating refrence:",ref.id)
 736            collection.document(ref.id).update(ar.as_dict()) # 1 query
 737        else:
 738            self.add_object(ar)  # 1 query
 739
 740    def update_all_average_ratings(self):
 741        """Updates the average rating of all listings
 742
 743        Uses 3 queries. + up to L updates
 744        """
 745        # i can do this in 3
 746        ratings = self.get_all_from(Rating())
 747        listings = self.get_all_from(Listing())
 748        current_averages = self.get_all_from(AverageRating())
 749
 750        # sort by listing
 751        ratings_per_listing:dict[str, list[Rating]] = {}
 752        for r in ratings:
 753            if r.ListingID in ratings_per_listing:
 754                ratings_per_listing[r.ListingID].append(r)
 755                continue
 756            ratings_per_listing[r.ListingID] = [r]
 757
 758        known_averages:dict[str, AverageRating] = {}
 759        for avg in current_averages:
 760            known_averages[avg.ListingID] = avg
 761        
 762        for l in listings: # for n listings
 763            try:
 764                ratings = ratings_per_listing[l.ListingID]
 765            except:
 766                rating = []
 767
 768            sum = 0
 769            count = len(ratings)
 770            for rating in ratings:
 771                sum += rating.Rating
 772            average = sum/count if count != 0 else 0
 773            if l.ListingID in known_averages:
 774                if average != known_averages[l.ListingID].AverageRating:
 775                    self.update_object(AverageRating(l.ListingID, average, count))
 776            else:
 777                self.add_object(AverageRating(l.ListingID, average, count))
 778            
 779    # Email functions
 780
 781    def send_code(self, user: User,code:Codes) -> bool:
 782        """Send an email to any user's email address
 783
 784        Args:
 785            user (User): The user we are sending the code to
 786            code (Codes): The code we are sending
 787
 788        Returns:
 789            bool: Whether the email was sent properly or not
 790
 791        Uses 0 queries.
 792        """
 793        subject = "RateMyHousingCode"
 794        body = f"Your code is {code.Code}"
 795        with open("src/Secrets2.json") as f:
 796            secrets = json.decoder.JSONDecoder().decode("".join(f.readlines()))
 797        sender = secrets["Sender"]
 798        password = secrets["Password"]
 799
 800        msg = MIMEText(body)
 801        msg['Subject'] = subject
 802        msg['From'] = sender
 803        msg['To'] = user.Email
 804        with smtplib.SMTP_SSL('smtp.gmail.com', 465) as smtp_server:
 805            smtp_server.login(sender, password)
 806            smtp_server.sendmail(sender, user.Email, msg.as_string())
 807            print(f"Message sent to {user.Email}")
 808            return True
 809        return False
 810    
 811    def verify_code(self, user: User,code: int) -> bool:
 812        """Verifies any code send from user is the valid code
 813
 814        Args:
 815            user (User): User who's code we are checking
 816            code (int): the code we want to varify
 817
 818        Returns:
 819            bool: true if the code is valid
 820
 821        Uses 2 queries.
 822        """
 823        if self.check_for_code(user):
 824            c = self.get_code_from_user(user)
 825            return c.Code == code
 826        return False
 827
 828    # check for x functions
 829    
 830    def check_for_username(self, username:str) -> bool:
 831        """Uses username to check for a valid user
 832
 833        Args:
 834            username (str): username to check
 835
 836        Raises:
 837            IOError: if not connected to database
 838
 839        Returns:
 840            bool: valid user or not
 841
 842        Uses 1 query.
 843        """
 844        if self.connected:
 845            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
 846            query = collection.where("Username","==",username)
 847            user = [doc.to_dict() for doc in query.get()]
 848            return len(user) >= 1
 849        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 850   
 851    def check_for_email(self, email:str) -> bool:
 852        """Checks to see if an email belongs to a user or not
 853
 854        Args:
 855            email (str): email to check
 856
 857        Raises:
 858            IOError: if not connected to database
 859
 860        Returns:
 861            bool: valid or not
 862
 863        Uses 1 query.
 864        """
 865        if self.connected:
 866            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
 867            query = collection.where("Email","==",email)
 868            user = [doc.to_dict() for doc in query.get()]
 869            return len(user) >= 1
 870        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 871
 872    def get_average_rating_ref(self, listing: Listing) -> DocumentSnapshot | None:
 873        """gets the average rating refrences from any listing
 874
 875        Args:
 876            listing (Listing): Listing to target
 877
 878        Raises:
 879            IOError: if not connected to database
 880
 881        Returns:
 882            DocumentSnapshot | None: the snapshot of the document that represents the average rating
 883        """
 884        if self.connected:
 885            collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
 886            query = collection.where("ListingID","==",listing.ListingID)
 887            test = query.get()
 888            if len(test) >= 1:
 889                return test[0]
 890            else:
 891                return None
 892        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 893    
 894    def check_for_code(self, user: User) -> bool:
 895        """checks if a user has a code or not
 896
 897        Args:
 898            user (User): The user to check for
 899
 900        Raises:
 901            IOError: If not connected to database
 902
 903        Returns:
 904            bool: true if code exists
 905
 906        Uses 1 query.
 907        """
 908        if self.connected:
 909            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Codes")
 910            query = collection.where("UserID","==",user.UserID)
 911            test = self._unwrap_query(query)
 912            if len(test) >= 1:
 913                return True
 914            else:
 915                return False
 916        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
 917    
 918
 919    # mod tools
 920    def find_orphend_data(self, data_type:T) -> list[T]:
 921        """finds all orphens in any particular datatype
 922
 923        Args:
 924            data_type (T): The datatype to find orphens for
 925
 926        Returns:
 927            list[T]: A list of all ophens
 928
 929        Uses 1+n queries.
 930        """
 931        orphens = []
 932        match data_type:
 933            case User():
 934                # dependent on password
 935                for user in self.get_all_from(User()):
 936                    try: self.get_pass_from_user(user)
 937                    except: orphens.append(user)
 938            case Rating():
 939                # dependent on user and listing
 940                for rating in self.get_all_from(Rating()):
 941                    try: self.get_user_from_rating(rating)
 942                    except: 
 943                        orphens.append(rating)
 944                        break
 945                    try: self.get_listing_from_rating(rating)
 946                    except: orphens.append(rating)
 947            case Landlord():
 948                # dependent on nothing
 949                pass
 950            case Password():
 951                # dependent on user
 952                for password in self.get_all_from(Password()):
 953                    try: self.get_object_by_id(password.UserID,User())
 954                    except: orphens.append(password)
 955            case Comments():
 956                # dependent on user and Listing
 957                for com in self.get_all_from(Comments()):
 958                    try: self.get_listing_from_comments(com)
 959                    except: 
 960                        orphens.append(com)
 961                        break
 962                    try: self.get_user_from_comments(com)
 963                    except: 
 964                        orphens.append(com)
 965            case Listing():
 966                # dependent on lanloard
 967                for listing in self.get_all_from(Listing()):
 968                    try: self.get_landlord_from_Listing(listing)
 969                    except: orphens.append(listing)
 970            case Codes():
 971                # dependent on user
 972                for code in self.get_all_from(Codes()):
 973                    try: self.get_object_by_id(code.UserID,User())
 974                    except: orphens.append(code)
 975            case AverageRating():
 976                # dependent on listing
 977                for average in self.get_all_from(AverageRating()):
 978                    try: self.get_object_by_id(average.ListingID,Listing())
 979                    except: orphens.append(average)
 980            case _:
 981                    pass
 982        return orphens
 983
 984    def has_missing_data(self, object:dict, data_type:DataObject)-> tuple[bool, list]:
 985        """checks any onee object to see if it has missing data or not
 986
 987        Args:
 988            object (dict): object to check
 989            data_type (DataObject): the datatype to check this to
 990
 991        Returns:
 992            tuple[bool, list]: returns wether the object has missing data or not and where the missing data is
 993
 994        Uses 0 queries
 995        """
 996        missing_keys = []
 997        result = False
 998        for field in fields(data_type):
 999            if field not in object:
1000                result = True
1001                missing_keys.append(field)
1002        return result, missing_keys
1003    
1004
1005
1006    # User relations
1007    # username -> user
1008    # email -> user
1009    # user -> password
1010    # User <-> lanloard
1011    # user <-> rating (many)
1012    # user <-> comments (many)
1013    # User -> Code
1014    def get_user_with_username(self, username:str) -> User:
1015        """With a username get a user 
1016
1017        Args:
1018            username (str): username to intaragate
1019
1020        Raises:
1021            TypeError: no user with that username
1022            IOError: if not connected with database
1023
1024        Returns:
1025            User: user with that username
1026
1027        Uses 1 query.
1028        """
1029        if self.connected:
1030            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
1031            query = collection.where("Username","==",username)
1032            user = self._unwrap_query(query)
1033            if len(user) == 1:
1034                user = user[0]
1035                return User.from_dict(user)
1036            else:
1037                raise TypeError(f"no user with the username: {username}")
1038        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1039    
1040    def get_user_with_email(self, email:str) -> User:
1041        """Get user with email
1042
1043        Args:
1044            email (str): email to look for
1045
1046        Raises:
1047            TypeError: no user with that email
1048            IOError: if not connected with database
1049
1050        Returns:
1051            User: user with that email
1052
1053        Uses 1 query.
1054        """
1055        if self.connected:
1056            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
1057            query = collection.where("Email","==",email)
1058            user = self._unwrap_query(query)
1059            if len(user) == 1:
1060                user = user[0]
1061                return User.from_dict(user)
1062            else:
1063                raise TypeError(f"no user with the email: {email}")
1064        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1065
1066    def get_pass_from_user(self, user:User) -> Password:
1067        """Get password from a user
1068
1069        Args:
1070            user (User): user in question
1071
1072        Raises:
1073            TypeError: no password with that userid
1074            IOError: if not connected to database
1075
1076        Returns:
1077            Password: the (hashed) password connected to the user
1078
1079        Uses 1 query.
1080        """
1081        if self.connected:
1082            password_list = self._get_document_using_id("Passwords",User(),user.UserID)
1083            if len(password_list) == 1:
1084                p = password_list[0]
1085                return Password.from_dict(p)
1086            else:
1087                raise TypeError(f"no password with userid: {user.UserID}")
1088        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1089    
1090    def get_landlord_from_user(self, user:User) -> Landlord:
1091        """get landlord connected to a particular user
1092
1093        Args:
1094            user (User): user to get LL of
1095
1096        Raises:
1097            TypeError: user has no LLid or no LL with that LLid
1098            IOError: if not connected to database
1099
1100        Returns:
1101            Landlord: landlord connected to that user
1102
1103        Uses 1 query.
1104        """
1105        if self.connected:
1106            if user.ConnectedLL ==  "":
1107                raise TypeError(f"User has no LLID")
1108            Landlord_list = self._get_document_using_id("Landlords", Landlord(),user.ConnectedLL)
1109            if len(Landlord_list) == 1:
1110                l = Landlord_list[0]
1111                return Landlord.from_dict(l)
1112            else:
1113                raise TypeError(f"no Landlord with LLID: {user.ConnectedLL}")
1114        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1115
1116    def get_ratings_from_user(self, user:User) -> list[Rating]:
1117        """get all ratings origionating from that user
1118
1119        Args:
1120            user (User): The user to inspect
1121
1122        Raises:
1123            IOError: if not connected to database
1124
1125        Returns:
1126            list[Rating]: a list of all ratings made by that user 
1127
1128        Uses 1 query.
1129        """
1130        if self.connected:
1131            ratings = self._get_document_using_id("Rating", User(),user.UserID)
1132            return [Rating.from_dict(r) for r in ratings]
1133        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1134    
1135    def get_comments_from_user(self, user:User) -> list[Comments]:
1136        """get comments made by a specific user
1137
1138        Args:
1139            user (User): user to inspect
1140
1141        Raises:
1142            IOError: if not connected to database
1143
1144        Returns:
1145            list[Comments]: list of all comment sconnected to this user
1146
1147        Uses 1 query.
1148        """
1149        if self.connected:
1150            coms = self._get_document_using_id("Comments", User(),user.UserID)
1151            return [Comments.from_dict(c) for c in coms]
1152        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1153    
1154    def get_code_from_user(self, user:User) -> Codes:
1155        """get a code from a user
1156
1157        Args:
1158            user (User): user to get code from
1159
1160        Raises:
1161            TypeError: code does not exsist
1162            IOError: if not connected to that database
1163
1164        Returns:
1165            Codes: the activation code for that user
1166
1167        Uses 1 query.
1168        """
1169        if self.connected:
1170            code_list = self._get_document_using_id("Codes", Codes(),user.UserID)
1171            if len(code_list) == 1:
1172                c = code_list[0]
1173                return Codes.from_dict(c)
1174            else:
1175                raise TypeError(f"no code with userid: {user.UserID}")
1176        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1177    
1178    # rating relationships
1179    # Rating <-> user
1180    # Rating <-> Listing
1181    def get_user_from_rating(self,rating:Rating) -> User:
1182        """get user from rating
1183
1184        Args:
1185            rating (Rating): rating to inspect
1186
1187        Raises:
1188            TypeError: invalid username
1189            IOError: if not connected to database
1190
1191        Returns:
1192            User: user who posted the rating
1193
1194        Uses 1 query.
1195        """
1196        if self.connected:
1197            users = self._get_document_using_id("Users",User(),rating.UserID)
1198            if len(users) == 1:
1199                u = users[0]
1200                return User.from_dict(u)
1201            else:
1202                raise TypeError(f"no username with userid: {rating.UserID}")
1203        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1204    
1205    def get_listing_from_rating(self,rating:Rating) -> Listing:
1206        """get listing connected to rating
1207
1208        Args:
1209            rating (Rating): rating to inspect
1210
1211        Raises:
1212            TypeError: invalid listing
1213            IOError: if not connect to database
1214
1215        Returns:
1216            Listing: listing that rating is on
1217
1218        Uses 1 query.
1219        """
1220        if self.connected:
1221            listings = self._get_document_using_id("Listing",Listing(),rating.ListingID)
1222            if len(listings) == 1:
1223                l = listings[0]
1224                return Listing.from_dict(l)
1225            else:
1226                raise TypeError(f"no listing with ListingID: {rating.ListingID}")
1227        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1228    
1229    # comments relationships
1230    # comment <-> user
1231    # comment -> comment
1232    # comment <-> listing
1233    def get_user_from_comments(self,comment:Comments) -> User:
1234        """get user from comments
1235
1236        Args:
1237            comment (Comments): the comment to investigate
1238
1239        Raises:
1240            TypeError: invalid userid
1241            IOError: if not connected to database
1242
1243        Returns:
1244            User: the user who made the comment
1245
1246        Uses 1 query.
1247        """
1248        if self.connected:
1249            users = self._get_document_using_id("Users",User(),comment.UserID)
1250            if len(users) == 1:
1251                u = users[0]
1252                return User.from_dict(u)
1253            else:
1254                raise TypeError(f"no username with userid: {comment.UserID}")
1255        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1256    
1257    def get_comments_from_comments(self,comment:Comments) -> Comments:
1258        """get a connected comment from comment a
1259
1260        Args:
1261            comment (Comments): the comment to start at
1262
1263        Raises:
1264            TypeError: no valid connected comment 
1265            IOError: if not connected to database
1266
1267        Returns:
1268            Comments: a comment that "replys" to the current comment
1269
1270        Uses 1 query.
1271        """
1272        if self.connected:
1273            com = self._get_document_using_id("Comments",Comments(),comment.ConnectedCommentID)
1274            if len(com) == 1:
1275                c = com[0]
1276                return Comments.from_dict(c)
1277            else:
1278                raise TypeError(f"no comment with comment: {comment.ConnectedCommentID}")
1279        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1280    
1281    def get_listing_from_comments(self,comment:Comments) -> Listing:
1282        """get the listing the comment is on
1283
1284        Args:
1285            comment (Comments): comment to invistigate
1286
1287        Raises:
1288            TypeError: listing is not real
1289            IOError: if not connected to database
1290
1291        Returns:
1292            Listing: listing comment is connected to
1293
1294        Uses 1 query.
1295        """
1296        if self.connected:
1297            listings = self._get_document_using_id("Listing",Listing(),comment.ListingID)
1298            if len(listings) == 1:
1299                l = listings[0]
1300                return Listing.from_dict(l)
1301            else:
1302                raise TypeError(f"no listing with ListingID: {comment.ListingID}")
1303        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1304    
1305
1306    # Listing relationships
1307    # listing -> landlord
1308    # listing <-> Rating (many)
1309    # listing <-> Comments (many)
1310    # listing -> Average rating
1311    def get_landlord_from_Listing(self, listing:Listing) -> Landlord:
1312        """get the landlord who made a specific listing
1313
1314        Args:
1315            listing (Listing): the listing in question
1316
1317        Raises:
1318            TypeError: invalid landlord
1319            IOError: if not connected to database
1320
1321        Returns:
1322            Landlord: landlord connected to this listing
1323
1324        Uses 1 query.
1325        """
1326        if self.connected:
1327            Landlord_list = self._get_document_using_id("Landlords", Landlord(),listing.LLID)
1328            if len(Landlord_list) == 1:
1329                l = Landlord_list[0]
1330                return Landlord.from_dict(l)
1331            else:
1332                raise TypeError(f"no Landlord with LLID: {listing.LLID}")
1333        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1334    
1335    def get_ratings_from_listing(self, listing:Listing) -> list[Rating]:
1336        """get all ratings from a specific listing
1337
1338        Args:
1339            listing (Listing): listing to investigate
1340
1341        Raises:
1342            IOError: if not connected to database
1343
1344        Returns:
1345            list[Rating]: a list of all ratings connected to that listing
1346
1347        Uses 1 query.
1348        """
1349        if self.connected:
1350            ratings = self._get_document_using_id("Rating", Listing(),listing.ListingID)
1351            return [Rating.from_dict(r) for r in ratings]
1352        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1353    
1354    def get_comments_from_listing(self, listing:Listing) -> list[Comments]:
1355        """get all comments that belong to a listing
1356
1357        Args:
1358            listing (Listing): the listing in question
1359
1360        Raises:
1361            IOError: if not connected to database
1362
1363        Returns:
1364            list[Comments]: a list of all comments talking about the server
1365
1366        Uses 1 query.
1367        """
1368        if self.connected:
1369            coms = self._get_document_using_id("Comments", Listing(),listing.ListingID)
1370            return [Comments.from_dict(c) for c in coms]
1371        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1372    
1373    def get_average_rating_from_listing(self, listing:Listing) -> AverageRating:
1374        """gets the average rating from a listing
1375
1376        Args:
1377            listing (Listing): listing to calculate
1378
1379        Raises:
1380            TypeError: non exsistent average rating
1381            IOError: if nor connected to database
1382
1383        Returns:
1384            AverageRating: average rating of that listing
1385
1386        Uses 1 query.
1387        """
1388        if self.connected:
1389            ars = self._get_document_using_id("AverageRating", AverageRating(),listing.ListingID)
1390            if len(ars) == 1:
1391                ar = ars[0]
1392                return AverageRating.from_dict(ar)
1393            else:
1394                raise TypeError(f"no AverageRating with ListingID: {listing.LLID}")
1395        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1396    
1397    # landlord rlationships
1398    # Landlord <-> User (many)
1399    # Landlord <-> Listing (many)
1400    def get_connected_users_with_landlord(self, landlord:Landlord) -> list[User]:
1401        """gets all users connected to that landlord
1402
1403        Args:
1404            landlord (Landlord): landlord in question
1405
1406        Raises:
1407            IOError: if not connected to database
1408
1409        Returns:
1410            list[User]: a full list of all users connected to a landlord
1411
1412        Uses 1 query.
1413        """
1414        if self.connected:
1415            users = self._get_document_using_id("Users", Landlord(),landlord.LLID)
1416            return [User.from_dict(u) for u in users]
1417        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
1418    
1419    def get_connected_listings_with_landlord(self, landlord:Landlord) -> list[Listing]:
1420        """get all listings the belong to a specific landlord
1421
1422        Args:
1423            landlord (Landlord): landlord in question
1424
1425        Raises:
1426            IOError: if not connected to database
1427
1428        Returns:
1429            list[Listing]: all listings conneceted to landlord
1430
1431        Uses 1 query.
1432        """
1433        if self.connected:
1434            listings = self._get_document_using_id("Listing", Landlord(),landlord.LLID)
1435            return [Listing.from_dict(l) for l in listings]
1436        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
connected: bool = False
fire_store: google.cloud.firestore_v1.client.Client
def connect_to_database(self): 
180    def connect_to_database(self):
181        """
182        Connects to database and sets things up internally.
183
184        Always run before any other commands
185        """
186        if not self.connected:
187            cred = credentials.Certificate("src/Secrets.json")
188            firebase_admin.initialize_app(cred)
189            self.connected = True
190            self.fire_store = firestore.client()
191            self.update_all_average_ratings()

Connects to database and sets things up internally.

Always run before any other commands

def get_object_by_id(self, id: str, object_type: ~T) -> ~T: 
319    def get_object_by_id(self, id:str, object_type:T) -> T:
320        """get any one object when given its id and its type
321
322        Uses 1 query
323
324        Args:
325            id (str): the id of the requested object
326            object_type (T): the type of the requested object
327
328        Raises:
329            TypeError: invalid id
330            IOError: not connected
331
332        Returns:
333            T: full object of the given type
334        """
335        if self.connected:
336            match object_type:
337                case User():
338                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
339                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", id).get()
340                    if len(documents) == 1:
341                        user  = documents[0].to_dict()
342                        if user != None:
343                            return cast(T, User.from_dict(user))
344                    raise TypeError(f"No one User with UserID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
345                case Password():
346                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Passwords") 
347                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", id).get()
348                    if len(documents) == 1:
349                        p = documents[0].to_dict()
350                        if p != None:
351                            return cast(T, Password.from_dict(p))
352                    raise TypeError(f"No one Password with UserID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
353                case Comments():
354                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Comments")
355                    documents = collection.where("CommentID", "==", id).get()
356                    if len(documents) == 1:
357                        c = documents[0].to_dict()
358                        if c != None:
359                            return cast(T, Comments.from_dict(c))
360                    raise TypeError(f"No one Comment with CommentId: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
361                case Landlord():
362                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Landlord")
363                    documents = collection.where("LLID", "==", id).get()
364                    if len(documents) == 1:
365                        l  = documents[0].to_dict()
366                        if l != None:
367                             return cast(T, Landlord.from_dict(l))
368                    raise TypeError(f"No one Landlord with LLID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
369                case Listing():
370                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Listing")
371                    documents = collection.where("LLID", "==", id).get()
372                    if len(documents) == 1:
373                        l  = documents[0].to_dict()
374                        if l != None:
375                            return cast(T, Listing.from_dict(l))
376                    raise TypeError(f"No one Listing with ListingID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
377                case Rating():
378                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Rating")
379                    documents = collection.where("RatingID", "==", id).get()
380                    if len(documents) == 1:
381                        r  = documents[0].to_dict()
382                        if r != None:
383                            return cast(T, Rating.from_dict(r))
384                    raise TypeError(f"No one Rating with RatingID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
385                case Codes():
386                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Codes")
387                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", id).get()
388                    if len(documents) == 1:
389                        c  = documents[0].to_dict()
390                        if c != None:
391                            return cast(T, Codes.from_dict(c))
392                    raise TypeError(f"No one Code with UserID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
393                case AverageRating():
394                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
395                    documents = collection.where("ListingID", "==", id).get()
396                    if len(documents) == 1:
397                        ar  = documents[0].to_dict()
398                        if ar != None:
399                            return cast(T, AverageRating.from_dict(ar))
400                    raise TypeError(f"No one AverageRating with ListingID: {id}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
401        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

get any one object when given its id and its type

Uses 1 query

Args: id (str): the id of the requested object object_type (T): the type of the requested object

Raises: TypeError: invalid id IOError: not connected

Returns: T: full object of the given type

def recursive_deletion( self, deleted_object: User | Rating | Landlord | Password | Comments | Listing | Codes | AverageRating) -> bool: 
403    def recursive_deletion(self, deleted_object:DataObject) -> bool:
404        """Recursivly removes all objects connected to the deleated object. including the origional object
405
406        Uses 1 query per object deleated.
407
408        Args:
409            deleted_object (DataObject): any data object
410
411        Raises:
412            TypeError: Invalid data type
413            IOError: not connected
414
415        Returns:
416            bool: weather deleation was sucessfull or not 
417        """
418        # recursive so it actually implements castcading removal.
419        if self.connected:
420            match deleted_object:
421                case User():
422                    # remove main object
423                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
424                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", deleted_object.UserID).get()
425                    if len(documents) == 1:
426                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
427                    else:
428                        return False
429
430                    # get connected items
431
432                        # passwords
433                    try:
434                        password = self.get_pass_from_user(deleted_object)
435                        self.recursive_deletion(password)
436                    except:
437                        pass
438
439                        # comments
440                    try:
441                        comments = self.get_comments_from_user(deleted_object)
442                        for c in comments:
443                            self.recursive_deletion(c)
444                    except:
445                        pass
446
447                        # ratings
448                    try:
449                        ratings = self.get_ratings_from_user(deleted_object)
450                        for r in ratings:
451                            self.recursive_deletion(r)
452                    except:
453                            pass
454                    
455                        # codes
456                    try:
457                        code = self.get_code_from_user(deleted_object)
458                        self.recursive_deletion(code)
459                    except:
460                            pass
461                    return True
462                case Password():
463                    # remove password and leave.
464                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Passwords")
465                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", deleted_object.UserID).get()
466                    if len(documents) == 1:
467                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
468                    else:
469                        return False
470                    return True               
471                case Rating():
472                    # remove rating and leave.
473                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Rating")
474                    documents = collection.where("RatingID", "==", deleted_object.RatingID).get()
475                    if len(documents) == 1:
476                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
477                    else:
478                        return False
479                    return True                
480                case Landlord():
481                    # remove main object
482                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Landlords")
483                    documents = collection.where("LLID", "==", deleted_object.LLID).get()
484                    if len(documents) == 1:
485                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
486                    else:
487                        return False
488
489                    # get connected items
490                        # Users
491                    try:
492                        users = self.get_connected_users_with_landlord(deleted_object)
493                        for u in users:
494                            self.recursive_deletion(u)
495                    except:
496                            pass
497                        # listings
498                    try:
499                        listings = self.get_connected_listings_with_landlord(deleted_object)
500                        for l in listings:
501                            self.recursive_deletion(l)
502                    except:
503                            pass
504                    return True
505                case Comments():
506                    # remove comment and leave.
507                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Comments")
508                    documents = collection.where("CommentId", "==", deleted_object.CommentId).get()
509                    if len(documents) == 1:
510                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
511                    else:
512                        return False
513                    return True
514                case Listing():
515                    # remove main object
516                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Listing")
517                    documents = collection.where("ListingID", "==", deleted_object.ListingID).get()
518                    if len(documents) == 1:
519                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
520                    else:
521                        return False
522
523                    # get connected items
524                        # comments
525                    try:
526                        comments = self.get_comments_from_listing(deleted_object)
527                        for c in comments:
528                            self.recursive_deletion(c)
529                    except:
530                            pass
531                        # ratings
532                    try:
533                        ratings = self.get_ratings_from_listing(deleted_object)
534                        for r in ratings:
535                            self.recursive_deletion(r)
536                    except:
537                            pass
538                        # rating average
539                    #average = 
540                    return True
541                case Codes():
542                    # remove code and leave.
543                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Codes")
544                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", deleted_object.UserID).get()
545                    if len(documents) == 1:
546                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
547                    else:
548                        return False
549                    return True
550                case AverageRating():
551                    # remove code and leave.
552                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
553                    documents = collection.where("ListingID", "==", deleted_object.ListingID).get()
554                    if len(documents) == 1:
555                        self.fire_store.recursive_delete(documents[0].reference)
556                    else:
557                        return False
558                    return True
559                
560                case _:
561                    raise TypeError(f"Id_source Not Valid Type: {deleted_object}, {type(deleted_object)}")
562        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

Recursivly removes all objects connected to the deleated object. including the origional object

Uses 1 query per object deleated.

Args: deleted_object (DataObject): any data object

Raises: TypeError: Invalid data type IOError: not connected

Returns: bool: weather deleation was sucessfull or not

def get_all_from(self, data_class: ~T) -> list[~T]: 
564    def get_all_from(self, data_class:T) -> list[T]:
565        """Get all data of any one particular type from the database. 
566
567        Uses 1 query
568
569        Args:
570            data_class (T): Any of the valid datatypes. dont use a real object just make a new constructor
571
572        Raises:
573            IOError: _description_
574
575        Returns:
576            list[T]: list of the same type as inputed
577        """
578        collection = ""
579        match data_class:
580            case User():
581                collection = "Users"
582            case Rating():
583                collection = "Rating"
584            case Landlord():
585                collection = "Landlords"
586            case Password():
587                collection = "Passwords"
588            case Comments():
589                collection = "Comments"
590            case Listing():
591                collection = "Listing"
592            case Codes():
593                collection = "Codes"
594            case AverageRating():
595                collection = "AverageRating"
596
597        if self.connected:
598            datalist = self._get_data(collection)
599            return cast(list[T], [data_class.from_dict(i) for i in datalist])
600        else:
601            raise IOError("Not Connected")

Get all data of any one particular type from the database.

Uses 1 query

Args: data_class (T): Any of the valid datatypes. dont use a real object just make a new constructor

Raises: IOError: _description_

Returns: list[T]: list of the same type as inputed

def add_object( self, object: User | Rating | Landlord | Password | Comments | Listing | Codes | AverageRating) -> None: 
603    def add_object(self,object:DataObject) -> None:
604        """Adds an object into the database
605
606        Uses 1 query
607
608        Args:
609            object (DataObject): any dataobject
610
611        Raises:
612            IOError: not connected to the database
613        """
614        collection = ""
615        match object:
616            case User():
617                collection = "Users"
618            case Rating():
619                collection = "Rating"
620            case Landlord():
621                collection = "Landlords"
622            case Password():
623                collection = "Passwords"
624            case Comments():
625                collection = "Comments"
626            case Listing():
627                collection = "Listing"
628            case Codes():
629                collection = "Codes"
630            case AverageRating():
631                collection = "AverageRating"
632        if self.connected:
633            result = self._push_data(object.as_dict(),collection)
634        else:
635            raise IOError("Not Connected")

Adds an object into the database

Uses 1 query

Args: object (DataObject): any dataobject

Raises: IOError: not connected to the database

def update_object( self, object: User | Rating | Landlord | Password | Comments | Listing | Codes | AverageRating) -> google.cloud.firestore_v1.types.write.WriteResult: 
637    def update_object(self, object:DataObject) -> WriteResult:
638        """Updates object in database. Use if the object was modified in any way
639
640        Uses 2 queries. One to get and 1 to update.
641
642        Args:
643            object (DataObject): object to update.
644
645        Raises:
646            TypeError: Object does not exsist in the database. Invalid id
647            IOError: not connected to the database
648
649        Returns:
650            WriteResult: write result. not usefull in most cases. ignore
651        """
652        if self.connected:
653            match object:
654                case User():
655                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
656                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", object.UserID).get()
657                    if len(documents) == 1:
658                        refrence = documents[0]
659                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
660                    raise TypeError(f"No one User with UserID: {object.UserID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
661                case Password():
662                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Passwords")
663                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", object.UserID).get()
664                    if len(documents) == 1:
665                        refrence = documents[0]
666                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
667                    raise TypeError(f"No one Password with UserID: {object.UserID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
668                case Comments():
669                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Comments")
670                    documents = collection.where("CommentID", "==", object.CommentId).get()
671                    if len(documents) == 1:
672                        refrence = documents[0]
673                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
674                    raise TypeError(f"No one Comment with CommentId: {object.CommentId}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
675                case Landlord():
676                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Landlord")
677                    documents = collection.where("LLID", "==", object.LLID).get()
678                    if len(documents) == 1:
679                        refrence = documents[0]
680                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
681                    raise TypeError(f"No one Landlord with LLID: {object.LLID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
682                case Listing():
683                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Listing")
684                    documents = collection.where("ListingID", "==", object.ListingID).get()
685                    if len(documents) == 1:
686                        refrence = documents[0]
687                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
688                    raise TypeError(f"No one Listing with ListingID: {object.ListingID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
689                case Rating():
690                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Rating")
691                    documents = collection.where("RatingID", "==", object.RatingID).get()
692                    if len(documents) == 1:
693                        refrence = documents[0]
694                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
695                    raise TypeError(f"No one Rating with RatingID: {object.RatingID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
696                case Codes():
697                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("Codes")
698                    documents = collection.where("UserID", "==", object.UserID).get()
699                    if len(documents) == 1:
700                        refrence = documents[0]
701                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
702                    raise TypeError(f"No one Code with UserID: {object.UserID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
703                case AverageRating():
704                    collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
705                    documents = collection.where(filter=FieldFilter("ListingID", "==", object.ListingID)).get()
706                    if len(documents) == 1:
707                        refrence = documents[0]
708                        return collection.document(refrence.id).update(object.as_dict())
709                    raise TypeError(f"No one AverageRating with ListingID: {object.ListingID}, there is {len(documents)} of them")
710        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

Updates object in database. Use if the object was modified in any way

Uses 2 queries. One to get and 1 to update.

Args: object (DataObject): object to update.

Raises: TypeError: Object does not exsist in the database. Invalid id IOError: not connected to the database

Returns: WriteResult: write result. not usefull in most cases. ignore

def update_average_rating(self, listing: Listing): 
712    def update_average_rating(self, listing: Listing):
713        """Updates the average rating of 1 specific listing
714
715        Uses 3 queries.
716
717        Args:
718            listing (Listing): any listing
719        """
720        # get all ratings for listing
721        # average
722        ratings = self.get_ratings_from_listing(listing) # 1 query, required
723
724        sum = 0
725        count = len(ratings)
726        for rating in ratings:
727            sum += rating.Rating
728        average = sum/count if count != 0 else 0
729
730        ar = AverageRating(listing.ListingID, average, count)
731        ref = self.get_average_rating_ref(listing) # 1 query
732        
733        if ref is not None:  
734            collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
735            print("average rating refrence:",ref.id)
736            collection.document(ref.id).update(ar.as_dict()) # 1 query
737        else:
738            self.add_object(ar)  # 1 query

Updates the average rating of 1 specific listing

Uses 3 queries.

Args: listing (Listing): any listing

def update_all_average_ratings(self): 
740    def update_all_average_ratings(self):
741        """Updates the average rating of all listings
742
743        Uses 3 queries. + up to L updates
744        """
745        # i can do this in 3
746        ratings = self.get_all_from(Rating())
747        listings = self.get_all_from(Listing())
748        current_averages = self.get_all_from(AverageRating())
749
750        # sort by listing
751        ratings_per_listing:dict[str, list[Rating]] = {}
752        for r in ratings:
753            if r.ListingID in ratings_per_listing:
754                ratings_per_listing[r.ListingID].append(r)
755                continue
756            ratings_per_listing[r.ListingID] = [r]
757
758        known_averages:dict[str, AverageRating] = {}
759        for avg in current_averages:
760            known_averages[avg.ListingID] = avg
761        
762        for l in listings: # for n listings
763            try:
764                ratings = ratings_per_listing[l.ListingID]
765            except:
766                rating = []
767
768            sum = 0
769            count = len(ratings)
770            for rating in ratings:
771                sum += rating.Rating
772            average = sum/count if count != 0 else 0
773            if l.ListingID in known_averages:
774                if average != known_averages[l.ListingID].AverageRating:
775                    self.update_object(AverageRating(l.ListingID, average, count))
776            else:
777                self.add_object(AverageRating(l.ListingID, average, count))

Updates the average rating of all listings

Uses 3 queries. + up to L updates

def send_code(self, user: User, code: Codes) -> bool: 
781    def send_code(self, user: User,code:Codes) -> bool:
782        """Send an email to any user's email address
783
784        Args:
785            user (User): The user we are sending the code to
786            code (Codes): The code we are sending
787
788        Returns:
789            bool: Whether the email was sent properly or not
790
791        Uses 0 queries.
792        """
793        subject = "RateMyHousingCode"
794        body = f"Your code is {code.Code}"
795        with open("src/Secrets2.json") as f:
796            secrets = json.decoder.JSONDecoder().decode("".join(f.readlines()))
797        sender = secrets["Sender"]
798        password = secrets["Password"]
799
800        msg = MIMEText(body)
801        msg['Subject'] = subject
802        msg['From'] = sender
803        msg['To'] = user.Email
804        with smtplib.SMTP_SSL('smtp.gmail.com', 465) as smtp_server:
805            smtp_server.login(sender, password)
806            smtp_server.sendmail(sender, user.Email, msg.as_string())
807            print(f"Message sent to {user.Email}")
808            return True
809        return False

Send an email to any user's email address

Args: user (User): The user we are sending the code to code (Codes): The code we are sending

Returns: bool: Whether the email was sent properly or not

Uses 0 queries.

def verify_code(self, user: User, code: int) -> bool: 
811    def verify_code(self, user: User,code: int) -> bool:
812        """Verifies any code send from user is the valid code
813
814        Args:
815            user (User): User who's code we are checking
816            code (int): the code we want to varify
817
818        Returns:
819            bool: true if the code is valid
820
821        Uses 2 queries.
822        """
823        if self.check_for_code(user):
824            c = self.get_code_from_user(user)
825            return c.Code == code
826        return False

Verifies any code send from user is the valid code

Args: user (User): User who's code we are checking code (int): the code we want to varify

Returns: bool: true if the code is valid

Uses 2 queries.

def check_for_username(self, username: str) -> bool: 
830    def check_for_username(self, username:str) -> bool:
831        """Uses username to check for a valid user
832
833        Args:
834            username (str): username to check
835
836        Raises:
837            IOError: if not connected to database
838
839        Returns:
840            bool: valid user or not
841
842        Uses 1 query.
843        """
844        if self.connected:
845            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
846            query = collection.where("Username","==",username)
847            user = [doc.to_dict() for doc in query.get()]
848            return len(user) >= 1
849        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

Uses username to check for a valid user

Args: username (str): username to check

Raises: IOError: if not connected to database

Returns: bool: valid user or not

Uses 1 query.

def check_for_email(self, email: str) -> bool: 
851    def check_for_email(self, email:str) -> bool:
852        """Checks to see if an email belongs to a user or not
853
854        Args:
855            email (str): email to check
856
857        Raises:
858            IOError: if not connected to database
859
860        Returns:
861            bool: valid or not
862
863        Uses 1 query.
864        """
865        if self.connected:
866            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
867            query = collection.where("Email","==",email)
868            user = [doc.to_dict() for doc in query.get()]
869            return len(user) >= 1
870        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

Checks to see if an email belongs to a user or not

Args: email (str): email to check

Raises: IOError: if not connected to database

Returns: bool: valid or not

Uses 1 query.

def get_average_rating_ref( self, listing: Listing) -> google.cloud.firestore_v1.base_document.DocumentSnapshot | None: 
872    def get_average_rating_ref(self, listing: Listing) -> DocumentSnapshot | None:
873        """gets the average rating refrences from any listing
874
875        Args:
876            listing (Listing): Listing to target
877
878        Raises:
879            IOError: if not connected to database
880
881        Returns:
882            DocumentSnapshot | None: the snapshot of the document that represents the average rating
883        """
884        if self.connected:
885            collection = self.fire_store.collection("AverageRating")
886            query = collection.where("ListingID","==",listing.ListingID)
887            test = query.get()
888            if len(test) >= 1:
889                return test[0]
890            else:
891                return None
892        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

gets the average rating refrences from any listing

Args: listing (Listing): Listing to target

Raises: IOError: if not connected to database

Returns: DocumentSnapshot | None: the snapshot of the document that represents the average rating

def check_for_code(self, user: User) -> bool: 
894    def check_for_code(self, user: User) -> bool:
895        """checks if a user has a code or not
896
897        Args:
898            user (User): The user to check for
899
900        Raises:
901            IOError: If not connected to database
902
903        Returns:
904            bool: true if code exists
905
906        Uses 1 query.
907        """
908        if self.connected:
909            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Codes")
910            query = collection.where("UserID","==",user.UserID)
911            test = self._unwrap_query(query)
912            if len(test) >= 1:
913                return True
914            else:
915                return False
916        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

checks if a user has a code or not

Args: user (User): The user to check for

Raises: IOError: If not connected to database

Returns: bool: true if code exists

Uses 1 query.

def find_orphend_data(self, data_type: ~T) -> list[~T]: 
920    def find_orphend_data(self, data_type:T) -> list[T]:
921        """finds all orphens in any particular datatype
922
923        Args:
924            data_type (T): The datatype to find orphens for
925
926        Returns:
927            list[T]: A list of all ophens
928
929        Uses 1+n queries.
930        """
931        orphens = []
932        match data_type:
933            case User():
934                # dependent on password
935                for user in self.get_all_from(User()):
936                    try: self.get_pass_from_user(user)
937                    except: orphens.append(user)
938            case Rating():
939                # dependent on user and listing
940                for rating in self.get_all_from(Rating()):
941                    try: self.get_user_from_rating(rating)
942                    except: 
943                        orphens.append(rating)
944                        break
945                    try: self.get_listing_from_rating(rating)
946                    except: orphens.append(rating)
947            case Landlord():
948                # dependent on nothing
949                pass
950            case Password():
951                # dependent on user
952                for password in self.get_all_from(Password()):
953                    try: self.get_object_by_id(password.UserID,User())
954                    except: orphens.append(password)
955            case Comments():
956                # dependent on user and Listing
957                for com in self.get_all_from(Comments()):
958                    try: self.get_listing_from_comments(com)
959                    except: 
960                        orphens.append(com)
961                        break
962                    try: self.get_user_from_comments(com)
963                    except: 
964                        orphens.append(com)
965            case Listing():
966                # dependent on lanloard
967                for listing in self.get_all_from(Listing()):
968                    try: self.get_landlord_from_Listing(listing)
969                    except: orphens.append(listing)
970            case Codes():
971                # dependent on user
972                for code in self.get_all_from(Codes()):
973                    try: self.get_object_by_id(code.UserID,User())
974                    except: orphens.append(code)
975            case AverageRating():
976                # dependent on listing
977                for average in self.get_all_from(AverageRating()):
978                    try: self.get_object_by_id(average.ListingID,Listing())
979                    except: orphens.append(average)
980            case _:
981                    pass
982        return orphens

finds all orphens in any particular datatype

Args: data_type (T): The datatype to find orphens for

Returns: list[T]: A list of all ophens

Uses 1+n queries.

def has_missing_data( self, object: dict, data_type: User | Rating | Landlord | Password | Comments | Listing | Codes | AverageRating) -> tuple[bool, list]: 
 984    def has_missing_data(self, object:dict, data_type:DataObject)-> tuple[bool, list]:
 985        """checks any onee object to see if it has missing data or not
 986
 987        Args:
 988            object (dict): object to check
 989            data_type (DataObject): the datatype to check this to
 990
 991        Returns:
 992            tuple[bool, list]: returns wether the object has missing data or not and where the missing data is
 993
 994        Uses 0 queries
 995        """
 996        missing_keys = []
 997        result = False
 998        for field in fields(data_type):
 999            if field not in object:
1000                result = True
1001                missing_keys.append(field)
1002        return result, missing_keys

checks any onee object to see if it has missing data or not

Args: object (dict): object to check data_type (DataObject): the datatype to check this to

Returns: tuple[bool, list]: returns wether the object has missing data or not and where the missing data is

Uses 0 queries

def get_user_with_username(self, username: str) -> User: 
1014    def get_user_with_username(self, username:str) -> User:
1015        """With a username get a user 
1016
1017        Args:
1018            username (str): username to intaragate
1019
1020        Raises:
1021            TypeError: no user with that username
1022            IOError: if not connected with database
1023
1024        Returns:
1025            User: user with that username
1026
1027        Uses 1 query.
1028        """
1029        if self.connected:
1030            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
1031            query = collection.where("Username","==",username)
1032            user = self._unwrap_query(query)
1033            if len(user) == 1:
1034                user = user[0]
1035                return User.from_dict(user)
1036            else:
1037                raise TypeError(f"no user with the username: {username}")
1038        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

With a username get a user

Args: username (str): username to intaragate

Raises: TypeError: no user with that username IOError: if not connected with database

Returns: User: user with that username

Uses 1 query.

def get_user_with_email(self, email: str) -> User: 
1040    def get_user_with_email(self, email:str) -> User:
1041        """Get user with email
1042
1043        Args:
1044            email (str): email to look for
1045
1046        Raises:
1047            TypeError: no user with that email
1048            IOError: if not connected with database
1049
1050        Returns:
1051            User: user with that email
1052
1053        Uses 1 query.
1054        """
1055        if self.connected:
1056            collection = self.fire_store.collection("Users")
1057            query = collection.where("Email","==",email)
1058            user = self._unwrap_query(query)
1059            if len(user) == 1:
1060                user = user[0]
1061                return User.from_dict(user)
1062            else:
1063                raise TypeError(f"no user with the email: {email}")
1064        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

Get user with email

Args: email (str): email to look for

Raises: TypeError: no user with that email IOError: if not connected with database

Returns: User: user with that email

Uses 1 query.

def get_pass_from_user(self, user: User) -> Password: 
1066    def get_pass_from_user(self, user:User) -> Password:
1067        """Get password from a user
1068
1069        Args:
1070            user (User): user in question
1071
1072        Raises:
1073            TypeError: no password with that userid
1074            IOError: if not connected to database
1075
1076        Returns:
1077            Password: the (hashed) password connected to the user
1078
1079        Uses 1 query.
1080        """
1081        if self.connected:
1082            password_list = self._get_document_using_id("Passwords",User(),user.UserID)
1083            if len(password_list) == 1:
1084                p = password_list[0]
1085                return Password.from_dict(p)
1086            else:
1087                raise TypeError(f"no password with userid: {user.UserID}")
1088        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

Get password from a user

Args: user (User): user in question

Raises: TypeError: no password with that userid IOError: if not connected to database

Returns: Password: the (hashed) password connected to the user

Uses 1 query.

def get_landlord_from_user(self, user: User) -> Landlord: 
1090    def get_landlord_from_user(self, user:User) -> Landlord:
1091        """get landlord connected to a particular user
1092
1093        Args:
1094            user (User): user to get LL of
1095
1096        Raises:
1097            TypeError: user has no LLid or no LL with that LLid
1098            IOError: if not connected to database
1099
1100        Returns:
1101            Landlord: landlord connected to that user
1102
1103        Uses 1 query.
1104        """
1105        if self.connected:
1106            if user.ConnectedLL ==  "":
1107                raise TypeError(f"User has no LLID")
1108            Landlord_list = self._get_document_using_id("Landlords", Landlord(),user.ConnectedLL)
1109            if len(Landlord_list) == 1:
1110                l = Landlord_list[0]
1111                return Landlord.from_dict(l)
1112            else:
1113                raise TypeError(f"no Landlord with LLID: {user.ConnectedLL}")
1114        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

get landlord connected to a particular user

Args: user (User): user to get LL of

Raises: TypeError: user has no LLid or no LL with that LLid IOError: if not connected to database

Returns: Landlord: landlord connected to that user

Uses 1 query.

def get_ratings_from_user(self, user: User) -> list[Rating]: 
1116    def get_ratings_from_user(self, user:User) -> list[Rating]:
1117        """get all ratings origionating from that user
1118
1119        Args:
1120            user (User): The user to inspect
1121
1122        Raises:
1123            IOError: if not connected to database
1124
1125        Returns:
1126            list[Rating]: a list of all ratings made by that user 
1127
1128        Uses 1 query.
1129        """
1130        if self.connected:
1131            ratings = self._get_document_using_id("Rating", User(),user.UserID)
1132            return [Rating.from_dict(r) for r in ratings]
1133        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

get all ratings origionating from that user

Args: user (User): The user to inspect

Raises: IOError: if not connected to database

Returns: list[Rating]: a list of all ratings made by that user

Uses 1 query.

1143
1144        Returns:
1145            list[Comments]: list of all comment sconnected to this user
1146
1147        Uses 1 query.
1148        """
1149        if self.connected:
1150            coms = self._get_document_using_id("Comments", User(),user.UserID)
1151            return [Comments.from_dict(c) for c in coms]
1152        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

get comments made by a specific user

Uses 1 query.

def get_user_from_rating(self, rating: Rating) -> User: 
1181    def get_user_from_rating(self,rating:Rating) -> User:
1182        """get user from rating
1183
1184        Args:
1185            rating (Rating): rating to inspect
1186
1187        Raises:
1188            TypeError: invalid username
1189            IOError: if not connected to database
1190
1191        Returns:
1192            User: user who posted the rating
1193
1194        Uses 1 query.
1195        """
1196        if self.connected:
1197            users = self._get_document_using_id("Users",User(),rating.UserID)
1198            if len(users) == 1:
1199                u = users[0]
1200                return User.from_dict(u)
1201            else:
1202                raise TypeError(f"no username with userid: {rating.UserID}")
1203        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

get user from rating

Args: rating (Rating): rating to inspect

Raises: TypeError: invalid username IOError: if not connected to database

Returns: User: user who posted the rating

Uses 1 query.

def get_listing_from_rating(self, rating: Rating) -> Listing: 
1205    def get_listing_from_rating(self,rating:Rating) -> Listing:
1206        """get listing connected to rating
1207
1208        Args:
1209            rating (Rating): rating to inspect
1210
1211        Raises:
1212            TypeError: invalid listing
1213            IOError: if not connect to database
1214
1215        Returns:
1216            Listing: listing that rating is on
1217
1218        Uses 1 query.
1219        """
1220        if self.connected:
1221            listings = self._get_document_using_id("Listing",Listing(),rating.ListingID)
1222            if len(listings) == 1:
1223                l = listings[0]
1224                return Listing.from_dict(l)
1225            else:
1226                raise TypeError(f"no listing with ListingID: {rating.ListingID}")
1227        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

get listing connected to rating

Args: rating (Rating): rating to inspect

Raises: TypeError: invalid listing IOError: if not connect to database

Returns: Listing: listing that rating is on

Uses 1 query.

def get_user_from_comments(self, comment: Comments) -> User: 
1233    def get_user_from_comments(self,comment:Comments) -> User:
1234        """get user from comments
1235
1236        Args:
1237            comment (Comments): the comment to investigate
1238
1239        Raises:
1240            TypeError: invalid userid
1241            IOError: if not connected to database
1242
1243        Returns:
1244            User: the user who made the comment
1245
1246        Uses 1 query.
1247        """
1248        if self.connected:
1249            users = self._get_document_using_id("Users",User(),comment.UserID)
1250            if len(users) == 1:
1251                u = users[0]
1252                return User.from_dict(u)
1253            else:
1254                raise TypeError(f"no username with userid: {comment.UserID}")
1255        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

get user from comments

Args: comment (Comments): the comment to investigate

Raises: TypeError: invalid userid IOError: if not connected to database

Returns: User: the user who made the comment

Uses 1 query.

def get_comments_from_comments(self, comment: Comments) -> Comments: 
1257    def get_comments_from_comments(self,comment:Comments) -> Comments:
1258        """get a connected comment from comment a
1259
1260        Args:
1261            comment (Comments): the comment to start at
1262
1263        Raises:
1264            TypeError: no valid connected comment 
1265            IOError: if not connected to database
1266
1267        Returns:
1268            Comments: a comment that "replys" to the current comment
1269
1270        Uses 1 query.
1271        """
1272        if self.connected:
1273            com = self._get_document_using_id("Comments",Comments(),comment.ConnectedCommentID)
1274            if len(com) == 1:
1275                c = com[0]
1276                return Comments.from_dict(c)
1277            else:
1278                raise TypeError(f"no comment with comment: {comment.ConnectedCommentID}")
1279        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

get a connected comment from comment a

Args: comment (Comments): the comment to start at

Raises: TypeError: no valid connected comment IOError: if not connected to database

Returns: Comments: a comment that "replys" to the current comment

Uses 1 query.

def get_listing_from_comments(self, comment: Comments) -> Listing: 
1281    def get_listing_from_comments(self,comment:Comments) -> Listing:
1282        """get the listing the comment is on
1283
1284        Args:
1285            comment (Comments): comment to invistigate
1286
1287        Raises:
1288            TypeError: listing is not real
1289            IOError: if not connected to database
1290
1291        Returns:
1292            Listing: listing comment is connected to
1293
1294        Uses 1 query.
1295        """
1296        if self.connected:
1297            listings = self._get_document_using_id("Listing",Listing(),comment.ListingID)
1298            if len(listings) == 1:
1299                l = listings[0]
1300                return Listing.from_dict(l)
1301            else:
1302                raise TypeError(f"no listing with ListingID: {comment.ListingID}")
1303        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

get the listing the comment is on

Args: comment (Comments): comment to invistigate

Raises: TypeError: listing is not real IOError: if not connected to database

Returns: Listing: listing comment is connected to

Uses 1 query.

def get_landlord_from_Listing(self, listing: Listing) -> Landlord: 
1311    def get_landlord_from_Listing(self, listing:Listing) -> Landlord:
1312        """get the landlord who made a specific listing
1313
1314        Args:
1315            listing (Listing): the listing in question
1316
1317        Raises:
1318            TypeError: invalid landlord
1319            IOError: if not connected to database
1320
1321        Returns:
1322            Landlord: landlord connected to this listing
1323
1324        Uses 1 query.
1325        """
1326        if self.connected:
1327            Landlord_list = self._get_document_using_id("Landlords", Landlord(),listing.LLID)
1328            if len(Landlord_list) == 1:
1329                l = Landlord_list[0]
1330                return Landlord.from_dict(l)
1331            else:
1332                raise TypeError(f"no Landlord with LLID: {listing.LLID}")
1333        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

get the landlord who made a specific listing

Args: listing (Listing): the listing in question

Raises: TypeError: invalid landlord IOError: if not connected to database

Returns: Landlord: landlord connected to this listing

Uses 1 query.

def get_ratings_from_listing(self, listing: Listing) -> list[Rating]: 
1335    def get_ratings_from_listing(self, listing:Listing) -> list[Rating]:
1336        """get all ratings from a specific listing
1337
1338        Args:
1339            listing (Listing): listing to investigate
1340
1341        Raises:
1342            IOError: if not connected to database
1343
1344        Returns:
1345            list[Rating]: a list of all ratings connected to that listing
1346
1347        Uses 1 query.
1348        """
1349        if self.connected:
1350            ratings = self._get_document_using_id("Rating", Listing(),listing.ListingID)
1351            return [Rating.from_dict(r) for r in ratings]
1352        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

get all ratings from a specific listing

Args: listing (Listing): listing to investigate

Raises: IOError: if not connected to database

Returns: list[Rating]: a list of all ratings connected to that listing

Uses 1 query.

def get_comments_from_listing(self, listing: Listing) -> list[Comments]: 
1354    def get_comments_from_listing(self, listing:Listing) -> list[Comments]:
1355        """get all comments that belong to a listing
1356
1357        Args:
1358            listing (Listing): the listing in question
1359
1360        Raises:
1361            IOError: if not connected to database
1362
1363        Returns:
1364            list[Comments]: a list of all comments talking about the server
1365
1366        Uses 1 query.
1367        """
1368        if self.connected:
1369            coms = self._get_document_using_id("Comments", Listing(),listing.ListingID)
1370            return [Comments.from_dict(c) for c in coms]
1371        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

Returns: list[Comments]: a list of all comments talking about the server

def get_average_rating_from_listing(self, listing: Listing) -> AverageRating: 
1373    def get_average_rating_from_listing(self, listing:Listing) -> AverageRating:
1374        """gets the average rating from a listing
1375
1376        Args:
1377            listing (Listing): listing to calculate
1378
1379        Raises:
1380            TypeError: non exsistent average rating
1381            IOError: if nor connected to database
1382
1383        Returns:
1384            AverageRating: average rating of that listing
1385
1386        Uses 1 query.
1387        """
1388        if self.connected:
1389            ars = self._get_document_using_id("AverageRating", AverageRating(),listing.ListingID)
1390            if len(ars) == 1:
1391                ar = ars[0]
1392                return AverageRating.from_dict(ar)
1393            else:
1394                raise TypeError(f"no AverageRating with ListingID: {listing.LLID}")
1395        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

def get_connected_users_with_landlord(self, landlord: Landlord) -> list[User]: 
1400    def get_connected_users_with_landlord(self, landlord:Landlord) -> list[User]:
1401        """gets all users connected to that landlord
1402
1403        Args:
1404            landlord (Landlord): landlord in question
1405
1406        Raises:
1407            IOError: if not connected to database
1408
1409        Returns:
1410            list[User]: a full list of all users connected to a landlord
1411
1412        Uses 1 query.
1413        """
1414        if self.connected:
1415            users = self._get_document_using_id("Users", Landlord(),landlord.LLID)
1416            return [User.from_dict(u) for u in users]
1417        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")

Raises: IOError: if not connected to database

Returns: list[User]: a full list of all users connected to a landlord

Uses 1 query.

1435            return [Listing.from_dict(l) for l in listings]
1436        raise IOError("Not Connected to Database")
